## PAGE 00001: (2026x Refresh1) 3DEXPERIENCE platform troubleshooting

- page_id: `304003686`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003686/2026x+Refresh1+3DEXPERIENCE+platform+troubleshooting
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:40.600+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page:**

4

##### Connectivity issues when the certificate is not trusted

If you cannot connect to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform because the certificate is not trusted, you need to export the certificate from the platform and then import it to the JRE trusted certificates keystore used to run your modeling tool.

To download the certificate file using Microsoft Edge

1. Open the **3D**EXPERIENCE p latform you want to download the certificate from.
2. On the left side of the address bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='ms_edge_icon.png'] .
3. Select Connection is secure and click [ATTACHMENT filename='certificate_icon.png'] .
4. In the Certificate Viewer window, select the Details tab.
5. Select the certificate and click the Export button. [ATTACHMENT filename='microsoft_edge_certificate_viewer.png']

To download the certificate file using Google Chrome

1. Open the **3D**EXPERIENCE p latform you want to download the certificate from.
2. On the left side of the address bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='chrome_icon.png'] and select Connection is secure > Certificate is valid .
3. In the Certificate Viewer window, select the Details tab.
4. Select the certificate and click the Export button. [ATTACHMENT filename='chrome_certificate_viewer.png']

To download the certificate file using sing Mozilla Firefox

1. Open the **3D**EXPERIENCE p latform you want to download the certificate from.
2. On the left side of the address bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='firefox_icon.png'] and select Connection secure > More information.
3. In the Security tab of the Page Info window, click the View Certificate button. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Scroll down to the **Miscellaneous**section and click **PEM (cert)** to download the certificate. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To install the certificate to JRE

1. Open the command prompt as an administrator.
2. Go to the JRE security directory located in <install_root>\jre\lib\security .
3. Copy the certificate file to the directory.
4. Run the following keytool command:
  - On Windows shell]]>
  - On Mac shell.pem]]>

- https://www.grim.se/guide/jre-cert
- https://stackoverflow.com/questions/25951602/adding-an-ssl-certificate-to-jre-in-order-to-access-https-sites

##### A used project cannot be found

If you try to open a project and get an error saying that a used project or projects are missing, this means that you either do not have sufficient permissions to access the used project or it was deleted.

You can still choose toopen the main project, but the used project or projects will be removed with all references to them:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In cases where the used project is not missing but cannot be opened because of insufficient permissions, we do not recommend cutting references as it may lead to data loss!

##### Project editing is disabled

When you open a project, you may see a balloon notification that project editing is disabled and the project is in read-only mode:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In such cases, check the following:

- if you have access to the collaborative space,
- if the project is shared with you; if so, is it shared with writing access?
- if you have access to write to set the maturity state of the opened revision

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004177 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00002: (2026x Refresh1) Accept Changes or Reject Changes

- page_id: `304004177`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304004177/2026x+Refresh1+Accept+Changes+or+Reject+Changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:12:16.383+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Used Project Switch Impact Analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Accept****Changes** button switches the used project version to the selected one. You will be warned if the selected version will cause or leave unresolved references.

**Reject Changes** restores the project to the state before invoking the Impact analysis with all of the local changes you had as well. The same appliesfor**X** (closing the dialog).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The <strong>Accept</strong><strong> Changes</strong> button switches the used project version to the selected one. You will be warned if the selected version will cause or leave unresolved references. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Reject Changes</strong> restores the project to the state before invoking the Impact analysis with all of the local changes you had as well. The same applies </span>for<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <strong>X</strong> (closing the dialog).</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003638 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00003: (2026x Refresh1) Adding projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `304003638`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003638/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+projects+to+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:38.004+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you start a collaboration session, you can add new projects to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform in one of the following ways:

- .
- Add a local project to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform .

To be able to add projects to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you must [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''] first.

##### Creating projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform

This section explains how to create a new project in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform from scratch.

To create a project in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Collaborate > New Server Project .
  - In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Collaborate > Projects and click the New button in the open dialog.
2. On the left side of the New Server Project dialog, select the kind of project you want to create.
3. In the Name box, type the project name and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_server_project_dialog.png']

##### Adding local projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform

This section explains how to add a local project to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

To add a local project to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. Open the local project you want to add to the 3D EXPERIENCE planform.
2. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Add Project to Server .
3. In the Name box of the open dialog, optionally change the name of the project.
4. In the **Comment** box, optionally add an explanation or note about the project. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you want to prevent the automatic creation of[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MT']in your project, click **More** in the **Add Project to the Server** dialog and clear the **Maintain Mount Points of Used Projects** check box. Have in mind that Mount relationships keep used projects in the same location as in the local project.
5. Click the Add button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<strong>Add Project to Server</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Name</strong> box of the open dialog, optionally change the name of the project.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Comment</strong> box, optionally add an explanation or note about the project.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_local_project_to_server.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1f1eaee-7d11-448a-add5-cf6767f75b99"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you want to prevent the automatic creation of </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Mount relationships]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> in your project, click <strong>More</strong> in the <strong>Add Project to the Server</strong> dialog and clear the <strong>Maintain Mount Points of Used Projects</strong> check box. Have in mind that Mount relationships keep used projects in the same location as in the local project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</li></ol>

## PAGE 00004: (2026x Refresh1) Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `304003824`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003824/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+projects+to+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:50.334+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

147331079

147331081

147331080

Projects can be added to Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) in one of the following ways:

- 
- 

createTo create a project in TWCloud from scratch

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. In the Online Projects tab, click the New button. The New Server Project dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_server_project.png']
3. In the Name box, type a new project name.
4. Next to the **Category**box, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the category in which you want to store your project.
  - To create a new category, select the Create Category button in the Select Category dialog: [ATTACHMENT filename='create_category_button.png']
5. Click OK .

addTo add a local project to TWCloud

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Starting a collaboration session' space=''] .
2. Open the local project you want to add to TWCloud.
3. From the Collaborate menu, choose Add Project to Server . The Add Project to the Server dialog opens.
4. If you want to give the project a new name, type the name directly in the Name box when it is added to the server.
5. Next to the**Category** box, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the category in which you want to store your project.
6. If you have some explanations or notes about this project, provide them in the Comment box.
7. usages If the project has no used projects, or if it has some that you want to be added to the server, skip this step. If you don't want any of the used projects to be added, do the following:
  1. Click More in the Add Project to the Server dialog for more options.
  2. Click the Local Projects tab. The list of used local projects opens.
  3. In the **Action** column of each local project that you don't want to add to the server, select **Leave as Local Project**. If you exclude all or some of the used projects from the addition to TWCloud (see step #7), you can alwayslink them to the project later. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] You can disable the **Maintain Mount Points of Used Projects**option in the **Add Project to the Server** dialog to prevent the automatic creation of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MED'] in your project.
8. Click Add . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. The project is now added to TWCloud.

When the project is added to the server:

- The title of the client application, e.g., MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, window displays #1 next to the project name. This is the initial project version number. [ATTACHMENT filename='ccs_solutions_in_modeling_tool.png']
- The contents of the Model Browser are [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Locking model for edit' space=''] . The model is unlocked.
- If the project is added to the used projects, you can see them in the Model Browser under the root package and under Project Usages . [ATTACHMENT filename='maintain_mount_points_example_.png']
- To view the project, select Collaborate > Recent Server Projects.

147331078

The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Online Projects</strong> tab, click the <strong>New</strong> button. The <strong>New Server Project</strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_server_project.png" /></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Name</strong> box, type a new project name.</li><li><p>Next to the <strong>Category </strong>box, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dots_button.png" /></ac:image> and select the category in which you want to store your project.</p><ul><li>To create a new category, select the<strong> Create Category</strong> button in the <strong>Select Category</strong> dialog:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_category_button.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1932dd24-509d-4a56-b8a6-5ad85677693b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">add</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add a local project to TWCloud</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Starting a collaboration session" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Log in to TWCloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Open the local project you want to add to TWCloud.</li><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Add Project to Server</strong>. The <strong>Add Project to the Server</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>If you want to give the project a new name, type the name directly in the <strong>Name</strong> box when it is added to the server.</li><li><p>Next to the<strong> Category</strong> box, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dots_button.png" /></ac:image> and select the category in which you want to store your project.</p></li><li>If you have some explanations or notes about this project, provide them in the <strong>Comment</strong> box.</li><li><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3996e67-3bba-4b35-bf71-f6aca701dfb4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">usages</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>If the project has no used projects, or if it has some that you want to be added to the server, skip this step. If you don't want any of the used projects to be added, do the following:<br /><ol><li>Click <strong>More </strong>in the <strong>Add Project to the Server</strong> dialog for more options.</li><li>Click the <strong>Local Projects</strong> tab. The list of used local projects opens.</li><li><p>In the <strong>Action</strong> column of each local project that you don't want to add to the server, select <strong>Leave as Local Project</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02eebe5b-43a3-4ec7-8542-92c9f646e3b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you exclude all or some of the used projects from the addition to TWCloud (see step #7), you can always<span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"> link them to the project</span></span> later.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_project_to_server.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36cc3aff-c113-4851-a765-d37b00a1e83d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can disable the <strong>Maintain Mount Points of Used Projects </strong>option in the <strong>Add Project to the Server</strong> dialog to prevent the automatic creation of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Mount relationships]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in your project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>Click <strong>Add</strong>.<br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_project_to_the_server.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p>The project is now added to TWCloud.</p></li></ol><p>When the project is added to the server:</p><ul><li>The title of the client application, e.g., MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, window displays<em>#1</em> next to the project name. This is the initial project version number.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ccs_solutions_in_modeling_tool.png" /></ac:image></li><li>The contents of the Model Browser are <ac:link ac:anchor="represent"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[gray]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The model is unlocked.</li><li>If the project is added to the used projects, you can see them in the Model Browser under the root package and under <em>Project Usages</em>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="maintain_mount_points_example_.png" /></ac:image></li><li>To view the project, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Recent Server Projects.</strong> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="135979c6-91f1-456d-b192-3498bd4ea86b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147331078</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating and managing categories" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003700 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00005: (2026x Refresh1) Adding standard profiles to 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `304003700`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003700/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+standard+profiles+to+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:41.338+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > (2026x Refresh1) Adding projects to 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Standard profiles should be stored in a public collaborative space everyone can access. If you try to add a project that requires a standard profile that does not exist on the server, the standard profile is automatically imported to the server. We highly recommend moving such standard profiles to a collaborative space dedicated to standard profiles.

##### Creating public collaborative spaces

We recommend that administrators responsible for managing MBSE on the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform create a public collaborative space for standard profiles. All users should have viewing permission for this space, but the editing permission should be reserved for the administrator.

To create a public collaborative space

1. On the 3D EXPERIENCE platform, click [ATTACHMENT filename='compass_icon.png'] and find Collaborative Spaces Control Center under My Apps .
2. Click Create Collaborative Space .
3. In the open dialog, enter the owner’s name (if it is not the current user).
4. In the Title box, the space title.
5. In the Description box, enter an optional space description.
6. In the Family drop-down box, select Design - Collaborative storage to manage any kind of content .
7. In the Visibility drop-down box, select Public - Content visible to all users .
8. Click the Create button. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_collaborative_space_dialog.png']

##### Adding standard profiles to the 3DEXPERIENCE platform

Once the collaborative space is created, users can add standard profiles to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

- Before starting work with the 3D EXPERIENCE platform, it is recommended to add all standard profiles provided in the modeling tool installation.
- Members of the same team should use a modeling tool with the same plugins.

To add standard profiles to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']Log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform where the collaborative space for standard profiles was created.
2. In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > Switch Platform/Credentials .
3. In the Credentials drop-down box of the open dialog, select the collaborative space. [ATTACHMENT filename='switching_to_public_space.png']
4. Click OK.
5. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Migrate Projects to Version <version number> .
6. In the **Show** drop-down menu of the open dialog, select **Standard Profiles**. It will display all standard profiles on the server and your modeling tool installation.****
7. Select the standard profiles you want to add or click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_all_button.png'] on the left side of the dialog to select all standard profiles. To ensure the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform is properly configured, we recommend selecting all available standard profiles. [ATTACHMENT filename='migrating_standard_profiles.png']
8. Click OK .

Following these steps will add the new standard profiles from your modeling tool installation to the platform and update any existing profiles to the version of your modeling tool. Once standard profiles are added to a public collaborative space, all newly created and added projects will use standard profiles from that space. If a profile is not added automatically, you can add it as a standalone project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Once standard profiles are added to a public collaborative space, all newly created and added projects will use standard profiles from that space. If a profile is not added automatically, you can add it as a standalone project.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003931 space=CM version=2 -->
## PAGE 00006: (2026x Refresh1) Branching projects

- page_id: `304003931`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003931/2026x+Refresh1+Branching+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:23:18.993+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to have the Project Merge plugin license key to use the merge functionality.

#### NOTE: Permissions

Permissions

To create, remove, rename, archive, or unarchive a branch, you need to have the [**Administer Resources** permission](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/?contextKey=permissions&version=2026x Refresh1&variant=default) assigned.

Project branching duplicates versions of a project and enables users to work simultaneously on them. Branching increases productivity for those, who have several development teams working on a single product and have to merge several development branches before releasing the product.

Branches can be created from any version of a Teamwork Cloud project or its branch. The branch version holds the information about the project version it is derived from. Every branch version can be tagged. Two versions of the same of different project branch can be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comparing projects' space='MT']and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space='MT']into a single one.

Projects that have usages can be branched *with* or *without* these usages. In the later case, the branch version of the project references versions of the projects used in the project, which that branch is derived from (it can be the latest version or some specific version selected by the user).

To create a project branch

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Select a project you want to branch.
3. On the toolbar of the Manage Projects dialog, click the Select Branches button [ATTACHMENT filename='select_branches.png'] . The Select Branch dialog opens.
4. In the open dialog, click the Edit Branches button. The Edit Branches dialog opens.
5. Select a project version you want to branch.
6. Click the Create Branch button. The Create Branch dialog opens.
7. In the open dialog, type the branch name and comment. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_a_branch.png']
8. Click OK . The new branch is created, and the dialog closes.
9. The **Edit Branches** dialog displays the new branch of the project. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Search barUse the search bar to filter projects according to:Project Version (together with the commit tags)AuthorComment
10. Click OK . The dialog closes.
11. If you want to open the newly created branch, do the following:
  1. Select it in the Select Branch dialog and click OK . The dialog closes.
  2. In the Manage Projects dialog, see that branch instead of the branched project.
  3. Click Open . The branch opens.

##### Archived branch

The branches that are not being worked with anymore and are only maintained for historical purposes can be hidden from users eyes.

Performance and disk space are not affected because archived branches are hidden only graphically.

To archive a project branch

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Select a project you want to archive branch.
3. On the toolbar of the Manage Projects dialog, click the Select Branches button [ATTACHMENT filename='select_branches.png'] . The Select Branch dialog opens.
4. In the open dialog, click the Edit Branches button. The Edit Branches dialog opens.
5. Right-click the branch you want to archive and, on the shortcut menu, click **Mark as Archived.**

The archived branches can be recognized from the grayed out text. Same way you can uncheck archived branch and it will be no more archived.

Project branches are archived globally.

To hide or show archived branches from the dialog

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and deselect Show Archived Branches , to hide archived branches.
- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and select Show Archived Branches , to show archived branches.

This action can be done in **Edit Branches**and **Select branch**dialogs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30548dd4-a918-4e75-9af9-01843c2bd473"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You need to have the Project Merge plugin license key to use the merge functionality. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="667360c9-efaa-48f6-9a25-dcc93de61ad7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Permissions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create, remove, rename, archive, or unarchive a branch, you need to have the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/?contextKey=permissions&amp;version=2026x Refresh1&amp;variant=default"><strong>Administer Resources</strong> permission</a> assigned.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Project branching duplicates versions of a project and enables users to work simultaneously on them. The branch version holds the information about the project version it is derived from. Every branch version can be tagged. Two versions of the same of different project branch can be <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Comparing projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[compared ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Model Merge" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[merged ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>into a single one.</p><p>Projects that have usages can be branched <em>with</em> or <em>without</em> these usages. In the later case, the branch version of the project references versions of the projects used in the project, which that branch is derived from (it can be the latest version or some specific version selected by the user).</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a project branch</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select a project you want to branch.</li><li>On the toolbar of the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog, click the Select Branches button <ac:image ac:title="Select Branches" ac:alt="Select Branches"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_branches.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Create Branch</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the open dialog, type the branch name and comment.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Name and comment of the new branch" ac:alt="Name and comment of the new branch"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_a_branch.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The new branch is created, and the dialog closes.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The <strong>Edit Branches</strong> dialog displays the new branch of the project.<br /><ac:image ac:title="New branch in the Edit Branches dialog" ac:alt="New branch in the Edit Branches dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_branch_in_the_edit_branches_dlg.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c459004-01a4-49e1-a5cd-e9e03857bb9d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Search bar</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use the search bar to filter projects according to:</p><ul><li>Project Version (together with the commit tags)</li><li>Author</li><li>Comment</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The dialog closes.</li><li>If you want to open the newly created branch, do the following:<ol><li>Select it in the <strong>Select Branch</strong> dialog and click<strong> OK</strong>. The dialog closes.</li><li>In the<strong> Manage Projects</strong> dialog, see that branch instead of the branched project.</li><li>Click <strong>Open</strong>. The branch opens.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Archived branch</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The branches that are not being worked with anymore and are only maintained for historical purposes can be hidden from users eyes. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce7b0aa8-302b-47fb-b6b7-a79e43a43b2b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Performance and disk space are not affected because archived branches are hidden only graphically.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span></p><p>To archive a project branch </p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select a project you want to archive branch.</li><li>On the toolbar of the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog, click the Select Branches button <ac:image ac:title="Select Branches" ac:alt="Select Branches"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_branches.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Select Branch</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the open dialog, click the <strong>Edit Branches</strong> button. The <strong>Edit Branches</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Right-click the branch you want to archive and,<span> on the shortcut menu, click <strong>Mark as Archived. </strong></span></li></ol><p>The archived branches can be recognized from the grayed out text. Same way you can uncheck archived branch and it will be no more archived. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fbc89716-c1c5-4c25-8955-3719a435dbcb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Project branches are archived globally.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To hide or show archived branches from the dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Click  <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and deselect <strong>Show Archived Branches</strong>, to hide archived branches.</li><li>Click  <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Show Archived Branches</strong>, to show archived branches.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d71dbcde-eac8-4d1e-aa50-5a76ea77e95c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This action can be done in <strong>Edit Branches </strong>and <strong>Select branch </strong>dialogs. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit_branches_archived_branch.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304004130 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00007: (2026x Refresh1) Change Sets

- page_id: `304004130`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304004130/2026x+Refresh1+Change+Sets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:12:15.697+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Reviewing project history
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Editing elements

1192448784

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Editing elements

1192448786

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Editing elements

1192448785

While editing your server project, you can track project modifications in the **Change Sets**panel, located right after the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MT']. A change set is a related group of elements, diagrams and symbol modifications in a project that are created or modified locally and not yet committed to the server. You may have several sets for your project and commit them one by one. This feature is useful when, for example, you are working on a server project and receive a request for an immediate change. You can suspend your changes, complete the immediate task and commit the changes, after which you can resume your workon the previously suspended change set. This also applies when you are working simultaneously on different tasks that are required to be delivered as separate commits.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A set of changes made to a server project.

#### NOTE: Editing elements

Editing elements

Ensure that you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Locking model for edit' space=''] to enable editing.

You can create as many change sets as you need. To organize and identify change sets, you may name each one. Only one change set can be active at a time. In order to create a new change set, all other change sets must be suspended. As shown in the preceding figure, colors on an element in the change set indicate whether the edit is an addition, modification or deletion. Also, you may [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comparing projects' space='MT'] between the base project and a project with modifications applied.

To manage an *active* change set

- Right click an active change set and select:
  - Commit - to commit changes to the server
  - Rename - to name a change set
  - Suspend - to suspend a change set for further editions.
  - Discard - to discard the local changes in a change set. Note: all changes will be removed and this action cannot be undone.
  - Tag version with Change ID after Commit - for the Change ID to be automatically reflected in the Project History.

To manage *suspended* change sets

- Right click a suspended change set and select:
  - Resume - to resume working with a suspended change set. Ensure that the active change set is suspended before resuming work on another change set.
  - Discard - to discard the local changes in a change set. Note: all changes will be removed and this action cannot be undone.

To compare a project

1. Select the active change set.
2. On the toolbar of the Change Sets panel, click [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diff_button.png'] . The list containing three types of comparison appears. Click Compare Projects .
3. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing comparison results' space='MT']**Difference Viewer**dialog opens showing changes in element property values as well as detailed information of directly related elements and new relationships.

##### Resuming change sets in Lock-Free Editing mode

In some cases, it may not be possible to resume the suspended change sets in the Lock-Free Editing mode without taking additional steps. The reason why this happens is the detection of conflicts among the suspended changes and present project version elements. In such a case, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing conflicting changes' space=''] before you can resume the changes and continue working on them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resuming suspended change sets.

The project version with the suspended change set has to be opened to review and confirm the update result of the conflicting changes. You can simply do so by clicking the **Review Conflicts** button in the warning that appears:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dialog asking to return to the project with the suspended change set version.

1192448783

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing comparison results' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003446 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00008: (2026x Refresh1) Changing usage type and scope

- page_id: `304003446`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003446/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+usage+type+and+scope
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:30.123+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When using server projects, you can choose from full usage, where an entire project is used, or partial usage, where you select the specific project scope you want to use. After using a project, you can change the usage type (full or partial) and scope as described below.

#### NOTE: Selecting usage scope

Selecting usage scope

If the elements in the selected usage scope reference elements outside the scope, these referenced elements will be matched with correspondingelements in the main project. If corresponding elements are not found in the main project and its current usages, all references to them will be removed from the selected scope.

For example, if the usage scope contains a part whose type is outside the scope, and the main project does not include that type, the reference to the type will be removed. As a result, the part in the used project will no longer have a type.

To minimize the risk of lost references, it is recommended to use all standard profiles of the used project in your main project beforehand.

##### Changing usage type

To change the usage type from full to partial

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select Options > Project Usages .
  - In the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, select the used project you want to change, click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_scope.png'] , and select Select Scope .
3. In the Select Scope (Technology Preview) dialog, s elect the project elements you want to use. Selecting a parent element automatically includes all of its owned elements in the usage scope. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click **OK**.
5. After the usage type is changed, click **OK** to close the **Project Usages** dialog.

To change the usage type from partial to full

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select Options > Project Usages .
  - In the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, select the project you want to change, click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_scope.png'] , and select Change to Full Usage . [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_usage_type_to_full.png']
3. After the usage type is changed, click **OK** to close the **Project Usages** dialog.

##### Changing usage scope

To change the usage scope

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select Options > Project Usages .
  - In the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, select the used project you want to change, click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_scope.png'] , and select Select Scope .
3. In the Select Scope (Technology Preview) dialog, change the usage scope by selecting and/or deselecting model elements . Selecting a parent element automatically includes all of its owned elements in the usage scope. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click **OK**.
5. After the usage scope is changed, click **OK** to close the **Project Usages** dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003739 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00009: (2026x Refresh1) Changing used project iterations

- page_id: `304003739`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003739/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+used+project+iterations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:43.742+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > (2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages in 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When a new used project iteration becomes available on the server, you may want to update the used project to the latest iteration. In addition, you can set up notifications about new used project iterations committed to the server.

##### Changing used project iterations

To change the used project iteration

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='MT'] .
2. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. In the Project Usages dialog, do one of the following:
  - Select a used project and click [ATTACHMENT filename='change_version_button.png'] .
  - Right-click a used project and select Change Iteration . [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_used_project_iteration.png']
4. In the Edit Branches dialog, select the project iteration you want to use and click OK .
5. When the iteration is changed, click OK to close the Project Usages dialog.

In the **Project Usages** dialog, used project iterations are shown in this format <*used iteration>/<latest iteration>*. For example, 2/3 means that project iteration 2[https://docs.nomagic.com/pages/resumedraft.action?draftId=111218408&draftShareId=1efcb639-bb31-4680-9fb3-f980b62c2a5c&](https://docs.nomagic.com/pages/resumedraft.action?draftId=111218408&draftShareId=1efcb639-bb31-4680-9fb3-f980b62c2a5c&)is used in your project, but the latest usage iteration on the server is 3.

##### Updating used projects to the latest iteration

To update used projects to the latest iteration

1. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Update_used_projects.PNG'] .
3. In the Update column of the open dialog, select the check boxes for the projects you want to update and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_used_projects.png']
4. Click OK to close the Project Usages dialog.

##### Setting notifications about new used project iterations

By default, you will get a notification aboutnew used project iterations every time you open the main project. You can change the setting for these notifications in the**Environment Options**dialog.

To change the notification settings for used projects

1. In the main menu, select**Options** > **Environment**.
2. In the**Environment Options**dialog, select the**Collaboration**option group.
3. Change the value of the **Notify About Newer Project Usages**option, to one of the following:
  - Open - to show notifications about new used project versions every time you open the main project.
  - Open, Update - to show notifications about new used project versions every time you open or update the main project.
  - **Never**- to disable notifications about new used project versions.
4. Click**OK**to close the**Environment Options**dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>When a new used project iteration becomes available on the server, you may want to update the used project to the latest iteration. In addition, you can set up notifications about new used project iterations committed to the server.</span></p><h3>Changing used project iterations</h3><p>To change the used project iteration</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, do one of the following:<ul><li>Select a used project and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_version_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project iterations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Right-click a used project and select <strong>Change Iteration</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_used_project_iteration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project iterations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Edit Branches</strong> dialog, select the project iteration you want to use and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>When the iteration is changed, click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.</li></ol><p><br />In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, used project iterations are shown in this format &lt;<em>used iteration&gt;/&lt;latest iteration&gt;</em>. For example, 2/3 means that project iteration 2<a class="toolbar-trigger aui-button aui-button-subtle" data-control-id="linkbrowserButton" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/pages/resumedraft.action?draftId=111218408&amp;draftShareId=1efcb639-bb31-4680-9fb3-f980b62c2a5c&amp;"> </a>is used in your project, but the latest usage iteration on the server is 3.</p><h3>Updating used projects to the latest iteration</h3><p>To update used projects to the latest iteration</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Update_used_projects.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project iterations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the <strong>Update</strong> column of the open dialog, select the check boxes for the projects you want to update and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_used_projects.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project iterations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.</li></ol><h3>Setting notifications about new used project iterations</h3><p style="">By default, you will get a notification about<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span> </span>new used project iterations every time you open the main project. You can change the setting for these notifications in the<span> </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span> </span>dialog.</span></p><p style=""><br /></p><p style="">To change the notification settings for used projects</p><hr style="" /><ol style=""><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the main menu, select<span> </span><strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the<span> </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span> </span>dialog, select the<strong><span> </span>Collaboration</strong><span> </span>option group.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Change the value of the <strong>Notify About Newer Project Usages </strong>option, to one of the following:</span><ul><li><strong>Open</strong><span> </span>- to show notifications about new used project versions every time you open the main project.</li><li><strong>Open, Update</strong><span> </span>- to show notifications about new used project versions every time you open or update the main project.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Never</strong><span> </span>- to disable notifications about new used project versions.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click<span> </span><strong>OK</strong><span> </span>to close the<span> </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span> </span>dialog.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003405 space=CM version=2 -->
## PAGE 00010: (2026x Refresh1) Changing used project versions

- page_id: `304003405`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003405/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+used+project+versions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:23:17.010+02:00`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### WARNING: Permissions

Permissions

To change the used project version, you must have:

- Read Resources, Edit Resources, Edit Resource Properties, and Administer Resources permissions on the main project and
- Read Resources permission on the used project whose version you want to change.

You can set an option to receive a notification when a new used project version appears. You may then choose to update the used project version to the latest version or keep working with the current one.

##### Setting notifications about version changes

By default, when you open your server project you will get a notification if there are used project versions later than the used one in your project that meet notification criteria. When this notification appears, click the **Update used projects** link and select the projects you want to update in the open **Update Used Projects** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can change the default notification settings to receive notifications when a server project is opened and when updates are done, as well as disable them if they are not necessary.

To set when to show notifications about newer used project versions

1. On the main menu, click **Options** > **Environment** to open the **Environment Options**dialog.
2. In the options group list, select **Collaboration**, then select the **Notify About Newer Project Usages**option, and specify the commands to be notified:
  1. 
    - on project opening and updating;
    - on project opening; or
    - never.

##### Notifications for any new version

If you do not need to specify a used project version with tags, you can choose to get notifications for any new version.

To get notifications for any new version

1. From the main menu, select **Collaborate** > **Project Usages** > **Project Usages**.
2. Select a used project and click to select the check box next to **Notify on**.
3. From the drop down list select **Notify for Any New Version**.
4. Click **OK** to close the dialog.

##### Notifications for new tagged versions

When you want to get notifications about updated used projects during your modeling process, you must define the used projects for routine version checking.You are allowed to specify certain commit tags for every used project. The modeling tool will show a notification when a new version of a used project with a specified commit tag is available.

To get notifications about changes to a certain tagged used project version

1. From the main menu, select **Collaborate** > **Project Usages** > **Project Usages**.
2. Select a used project and click to select the check box next to **Notify on**.
3. From the drop down list select**Notify on New Tagged Version**.
4. Click **Specify Tags**, near the drop-down list.
5. In the **Specify Tags**dialog, double click the first row. To add or delete rows, do one of the following:
  1. To add a row, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], or press Alt + Enter.
  2. To delete a row, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], or press Alt + Backspace.
6. Specify the tag, e.g., *2018Q*(tags are case insensitive), and then click **OK** to close the dialog. If you do not specify tags, the modeling tool will show a warning icon.It is recommended that tags specification be done in patterns, because the modeling tool searches for partial matches. See the example below for more information.ExampleAfter a new version of the used project is committed to the server with a tag that fully or partially matches the specified tag, you will be notified about updates of these tagged projects.Let’s say you specify these tags: 
*2018Q* 
*2019Q* 
*2020Q* 
*Major*New versions and notification status:New version without tags - no notificationNew version with the tag [ Major ] – notification about a new versionNew version with the tag [ 2018q1 ] – notification about a new versionNew version with tags [Release][Major] – notification about a new versionNew version with the tag [Release] – no notificationNew version with the tag [Q_2018Q2T] - notification about a new version
7. Click **OK** to close the dialog.

##### Updating used projects to the latest version

If a used project has tags specified for notification, only the version meeting the used projects notifications criteria (tags) will be shown in this dialog.

To update a used project to the latest version

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
2. Click the Update Used Projects button.
3. In the open Update Used Projects dialog, select the project you want to update. [ATTACHMENT filename='Update Used Projects_target version.png']
4. Wait while the version changes to the latest and click OK to close the dialog. The used project is automatically locked. The lock will be released after the project commit.

##### Changing used project versions

To change the used project version

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Locking model for edit' space=''] .
2. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. Select a used project.
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to change the version of a used project. Shortcut menuThis step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select **Change version.** [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the open dialog, select the used project version to switch to and click OK .
6. Wait while the version changes to the selected one and click OK to close the dialog.

Versions are displayed in the following format:*#<used version>/<latest version>.*

The term #1/3 denotes that 1 is the used version and 3 is the latest version.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003381 space=CM version=2 -->
## PAGE 00011: (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation

- page_id: `304003381`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003381/2026x+Refresh1+Client-Side+Collaborative+Modeling+Documentation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:23:16.454+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

lightgrey

This guide provides documentation for client-side collaboration on server projects stored in Teamwork Cloud or the 3DEXPERIENCE platform.

Team members can individually develop separate parts of a single model and then merge them together to form a complete model. In comparison to individual modeling, collaborative modeling is more effective and produces better results because each member employs his/her best skills and experience.

We offer you a server for your team's collaboration – [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']collaboration capabilities powered by **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

Both servers provide a repository for storing projects and users. They also support the management of users' permissions as well as sharing and versioning projects.

Projects stored in the server repository can be accessed through the network from multiple clients who have a modeling tool installed. Different users, depending on their role in a team or enterprise, can have different permissions to the projects.

The same model or even the same diagram can be accessed and modified in parallel. Every user may instantly obtain the newest version of the model as well as commit his/her own changes.

##### Basic concepts

For better understanding further material, get acquainted with basic concepts of collaborative modeling.

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Repository | A place for storing projects and users managed by Teamwork Cloud. |
| User | A collaboration team member, who has credentials to log into Teamwork Cloud and holds permissions to access one or more server projects. |
| Teamwork Cloud project | A project stored in Teamwork Cloud repository. |
| Server project | A common name for Teamwork Cloud projects. |
| Version | A snapshot of a project at a particular point in time. A new version of a project is created after successful commit of changes made in this project. All versions of the same project have unique numbers. |
| Comment | An optional description about changes in a project version. |
| Tag | Information about the status of a project version (for example, approved or initially tested) or other. |
| Commit | A process of sending the changes made in the project to the server. Each commit creates a new project version. |
| Author | A user who has committed a particular project version. |
| Update | A process of getting from the server the latest version of a project you are working with. |
| Category | A concept for grouping projects in a Teamwork Cloud repository. |
| Locked item | A part of the model that can be edited by the user, who has locked it. Other users cannot edit locked items. The locks can be released during the commit of a new version. |
| Server project usage | Another server project used in the project you are working with. It can be a library, profile or regular project. |
| Branch | A duplicate of a server project version. Branching allows the users to work in parallel on the same project version. |
| Trunk | A permanent branch of a server project that is a base for all other branches. It cannot be deleted. |

##### Scenario to get started

Independent of what server your team or enterprise uses, we offer you to follow these steps to get started with collaborative modelling:

1. Obtain user's credentials. Creating users and deciding the access rights of the users against projects is an administrative task. For more information, see [Teamwork Cloud User Guide](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/?contextKey=user-guide&version=2026x Refresh1&variant=Teamwork%20Cloud%20and%20Services).
2. Log into a server.
3. Add a project to the server and/or open it.
4. Lock the elements you want to edit (to prevent other users could not change the elements you are working with) and edit them. You can skip this step if you are working in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Lock-Free Editing mode' space=''].
5. Edit these elements and/or create new ones.
6. Commit the changes to the server so that other users could see these modifications.

## PAGE 00012: (2026x Refresh1) Cloning projects

- page_id: `304003921`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003921/2026x+Refresh1+Cloning+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:56.092+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

54100852

54100855

54100853

You can clone the latest version of your server project as a separate project or the latest version of a project branch as a separate branch. The cloned project can be used as a "template" to create a new project or as an independent base for new development. IDs of a cloned project and elements are reset automatically. Project permissions are reset as well; the project is available only for the user who has cloned the project. All references to used projects (if there are any) are maintained.

- You can only clone either the latest version of a project or the latest version of a project branch.
- You need at least double the amount of memory to clone a project in comparison to opening a project.

To clone a project or a branch

1. On the main menu, click **Collaborate**> **Projects**. The **Manage Projects** dialog opens.
2. To clone a project, select the project you want to clone. You can use the search bar to filter used projects fasterby name.
3. (Optional) To clone a branch of a chosen project, select the project and do one of the following: [ATTACHMENT filename='1_select_branch.png']
  - Click the Select Branch [ATTACHMENT filename='select_branches_button.png'] button.
  - Click the three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] button in the Branch section.
  - Right-click the project and select Select Branch . Select the branch you want to clone in the Select Branch dialog and click OK .
4. To clone the selected project or branch, do one of the following: [ATTACHMENT filename='2_clone_server_project.png']
  - Click the Clone button.
  - Right-click the project and select Clone Server Project .
5. The Clone Server Project dialog opens, wherein you need to type the name of the cloned project, select a category to save the project and/or add a comment. Click More , if you want to add a tag for the project.
6. (Optional) To clone with used projects, click**More**, select the**Used Projects**tab and check the**Clone Used Projects**check box. You can change the names of the cloned used projects in the**New Name**section once the**Clone Used Projects**check box is selected. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Cloned used projects do not include package permissions, hidden packages, and code engineering sets.
7. Click Clone when you are done.
8. Close the Manage Projects dialog.

Before using the cloned project with Teamwork Cloud web applications, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud' space=''].

54100851

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space='']

## PAGE 00013: (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration between disconnected teams

- page_id: `304003490`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003490/2026x+Refresh1+Collaboration+between+disconnected+teams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:31.313+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['disconnected-collaboration']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1177149143

1177149146

1177149144

Teamwork Cloud server allows for collaborating on projects, supporting multiple contractors who cannot work on the same Teamwork Cloud installation. There are two ways to collaborate on projects – using either the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using server project file format' space='']server project file format *.szip***or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using local project file format' space='']local project file format *.mdzip**.*

Read more about different ways to collaborate on projects in the following sections:

1177149142

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Saving a local copy of Teamwork Cloud project' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space='MT']

There are two ways to collaborate on projects – using either the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using server project file format" /><ac:link-body>server project file format <em>.szip</em></ac:link-body></ac:link><em> </em>or the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using local project file format" /><ac:link-body>local project file format <em>.mdzip</em></ac:link-body></ac:link><em>.</em></p><p>Read more about different ways to collaborate on projects in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="614d739d-186d-44cd-a020-9d85dfe18fa8" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="442bff62-59ea-48bb-b0a8-00397156cc11"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1177149142</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Saving a local copy of Teamwork Cloud project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Model Merge" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003605 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00014: (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `304003605`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003605/2026x+Refresh1+Collaboration+powered+by+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:36.085+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

CATIA Magic and No Magic modeling tools support collaboration on UML/SysML v1/UAF models in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform (the feature also known as Power'By). You can use a modeling tool to create and store your projects in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform. Moreover, different team members can work on the same project simultaneously, commit new project iterations to the platform, and update the project when new iterations become available.

There are two ways you can start a collaboration session in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform:

- Open a modeling tool and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform .
- Use the Design with CATIA Magic app in the 3D EXPERIENCE platform to open a modeling tool installed on your computer.

600400

To learn how to collaborate on projects in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, see:

- If collaboration powered by the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform is enabled, you cannot work with Teamwork Cloud.
- Unsupported plugins:
  - Model Obfuscator.
  - Cameo Concept Modeller. Contact [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support' space='SUP'] for the workaround.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can use a modeling tool to create and store your projects in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform. Moreover, different team members can work on the same project simultaneously, commit new project iterations to the platform, and update the project when new iterations become available.</p><p>There are two ways you can start a collaboration session in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform:</p><ul><li>Open a modeling tool and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>log in to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Use the <a href="https://help.3ds.com/2023x/english/dsdoc/CATMagcUserMap/CATMagc-c-ov.htm?contextscope=cloud">Design with CATIA Magic app</a> in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform to open a modeling tool installed on your computer.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05e9fe92-b3a8-4879-a7b6-76cce3f26998"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="http://youtube.com/watch?v=52ANuF6gOH0" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br />To learn how to collaborate on projects in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, see:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4b769c12-280d-4052-b68e-48138a1c9b91" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29d89163-9955-4e7c-a561-e0ce0574f2b5"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If collaboration powered by the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform is enabled, you cannot work with Teamwork Cloud.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Unsupported plugins:</span><br /><ul><li>Model Obfuscator.</li><li>Cameo Concept Modeller. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003661 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00015: (2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `304003661`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003661/2026x+Refresh1+Committing+changes+to+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:39.053+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After you finish editing a server project, you must commit the changes to the **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform so that other users working on the same project would be able to see them. When you commit project changes, new project data from your modeling tool is merged with the project stored on the**server**. As a result, a new project iterationis created.

To commit project changes to the **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Collaborate > Commit Changes to Server .
2. In the **Comment** box of the open dialog, optionally describe the model changes.
3. If you want to keep the locks on all diagrams, leave the option Keep locks selected.
4. If you want to view the local changes you are about to commit, click the**More**button. In the**Local Changes**tab, added elements are highlighted in green, deleted elements are highlighted in red, and modified or moved elements are highlighted in blue. [ATTACHMENT filename='commiting_project_to_server.png']
5. Click the Commit button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

When you commit project changes, new project data from your modeling tool is merged with the project stored on the </span><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"><strong>server</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. As a result, a new project iteration </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">is created.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To commit project changes to the <span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"><strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE </span>platform</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Commit Changes to Server</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Comment</strong> box of the open dialog, optionally describe the model changes.</p></li><li>If you want to keep the locks on all diagrams, leave the option <strong>Keep locks </strong>selected.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>If you want to view the local changes you are about to commit, click the </span><strong>More</strong><span> button</span><span>. In the </span><strong>Local Changes</strong><span> tab, added elements are highlighted in green, deleted elements are highlighted in red, and modified or moved elements are highlighted in blue.</span><br /><br /></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="commiting_project_to_server.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click the <strong>Commit</strong> button.</li></ol>
