<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003457 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00016: (2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `304003457`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003457/2026x+Refresh1+Committing+changes+to+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:30.435+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

After you finish editing a model, you need to commit the changes to the server so that other team members can see these changes when editing other parts of the model. When you commit changes to the server, new data from your modeling tool is merged with the project on the server repository. As a result, a new version of that project is created. Other team members must [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Updating changes from Teamwork Cloud' space=''] from the server to see the committed changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The workflow of modifying and committing server projects.

To commit a project to Teamwork Cloud

1. In the main menu. select Collaborate > Commit Changes To Server (or press Ctrl+K).
2. If you want to describe the changes you made, enter a comment in the **Comment** box of the **Commit Changes to the Server**dialog. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] above the **Comment** box to choose a comment from the recently used comments list.
3. If you want to keep locks on model elements, select the **Keep locks** check box. Your choice will be remembered. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Optionally, click More in the lower left corner of the dialog and do one or both the following:
  - .
  - .
5. Click the Commit button.

While the project is being committed, you will see a dialog with a progress bar and your position in the commit queue as shown below.

When multiple projects are committed to the server simultaneously, all users are put in a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) line resulting in quicker and more predictable project commit execution. If putting project commits in a queue does not work for your organization, your system administrator can for a particular Teamwork Cloud server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The commit progress dialog with your position in the commit queue.

##### Creating version tags

You can create tags for a project version when you commit it to the server. Version tags provide valuable information when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing historical versions of the project' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects' space='']. You can also use tags to trigger notifications when used project versions with specific tags are committed to the server. To learn more, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project versions' space=''].

To create a version tag and commit a project

1. In the main menu. select Collaborate > Commit Changes To Server (or press Ctrl+K).
2. In the lower left corner of the Commit Changes to the Server dialog, click More .
3. In the Version Tags tab, click the Add button.
4. In the open dialog, type the tag text and click OK . The tag is added to the Version Tags tab. You create several tags for one project version.Select the **Create major version** check box, to add the 'Major' tag to the project version. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_version_tags.png']
5. Click the Commit button.

##### Reviewing local changes before committing a project

You can review the local changes you have made to the project before committing it to the server.

To review local changes and commit a project

1. In the main menu. select Collaborate > Commit Changes To Server (or press Ctrl+K).
2. In the lower left corner of the Commit Changes to the Server dialog, click More .
3. Select the Local Changes tab and review local changes made to the project. Added elements are highlighted in green, deleted elements are highlighted in red, and modified or moved elements are highlighted in blue. [ATTACHMENT filename='reviewing_local_changes.png']
4. If you want to explore the changes of a certain element in more detail, select an element, click [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diff.png'] on the right side of the Local Changes tab, then select one of the following options:
  - Quick Diff - to quickly inspect the changes of element property values (such as documentation, type, or multiplicity).
  - Full Diff - to inspect the changes made to diagrams or element property values in more detail, as well as look at the properties of new relationships and directly related elements (such as new or modified attributes and operations).
  - Compare projects - to compare local project changes to the latest project version on the server. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing comparison results' space='MT'] .
5. Click the Commit button.

##### Disabling commit queues

If putting project commits in a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) line does not work for your organization, your system administrator candisable the commit queue mechanismfor a particular Teamwork Cloud server.

To disable commit queues

1. Open for editing the <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf file.
2. Add the following line to the file:
3. Restart the Teamwork Cloud server.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a35965f0-00b4-4e9f-b20d-b9aa2fae21dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>After you finish editing a model, you need to commit the changes to the server so that other team members can see these changes when editing other parts of the model. When you commit changes to the server, new data from your modeling tool is merged with the project on the server repository. As a result, a new version of that project is created. Other team members must <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Updating changes from Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update their projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the server to see the committed changes.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="committing_projects.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The workflow of modifying and committing server projects.</h6><p><br /></p><p>To commit a project to Teamwork Cloud</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu. select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Commit Changes To Server</strong> (or press Ctrl+K).</li><li><p>If you want to describe the changes you made, enter a comment in the <strong>Comment</strong> box of the <strong>Commit Changes to the Server </strong>dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0591a63-82a8-4b30-9fad-1613aa43fb02"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="choose_comment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> above the <strong>Comment</strong> box to choose a comment from the recently used comments list.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>If you want to keep locks on model elements, select the <strong>Keep locks</strong> check box. Your choice will be remembered.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="commit_project_to_the_server_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li>Optionally, click <strong>More</strong> in the lower left corner of the dialog and do one or both the following:<ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating version tags"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a tag for the new project version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Reviewing local changes before committing a project"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Review local changes before committing the project to the server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Commit</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />While the project is being committed, you will see a dialog with a progress bar and your position in the commit queue as shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a2af343-c8af-49f1-9f40-60594e0d4627"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">When multiple projects are committed to the server simultaneously, all users are put in a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) line resulting in <span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">quicker and more predictable project commit execution. If putting project commits in a queue does not work for your organization, your system administrator can <ac:link ac:anchor="Disabling commit queues"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[disable the commit queue mechanism]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for a particular Teamwork Cloud server.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="commit_queues.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The commit progress dialog with your position in the commit queue.</h6><h3>Creating version tags</h3><p>You can create tags for a project version when you commit it to the server. Version tags provide valuable information when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Reviewing historical versions of the project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reviewing project history]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creating project branches]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can also use tags to trigger notifications when used project versions with specific tags are committed to the server. To learn more, see <ac:link ac:anchor="Setting notifications about version changes"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project versions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Setting notifications about version changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a version tag and commit a project</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the main menu. select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Collaborate</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Commit Changes To Server</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> (or press Ctrl+K).</span></li><li>In the lower left corner of the <strong>Commit Changes to the Server </strong>dialog, click <strong>More</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Version </strong><strong>Tags</strong> tab, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</li><li>In the open dialog, type the tag text and click <strong>OK</strong>. The tag is added to the <strong>Version Tags</strong> tab.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3809347-b917-45e9-840b-3a87d6cedeea"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You create several tags for one project version.</li><li>Select the <strong>Create major version</strong> check box, to add the 'Major' tag to the project version.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_version_tags.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click the <strong>Commit</strong> button.</li></ol><h3>Reviewing local changes before committing a project</h3><p>You can review the local changes you have made to the project before committing it to the server.</p><p><br /></p><p>To review local changes and commit a project</p><hr /><ol><li><span>In the main menu. select </span><strong>Collaborate</strong><span> &gt; </span><strong>Commit Changes To Server</strong><span> (or press Ctrl+K).</span></li><li>In the lower left corner of the <strong>Commit Changes to the Server </strong>dialog, click <strong>More</strong>.</li><li>Select the <strong>Local Changes</strong> tab and review local changes made to the project.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="951aec04-8587-47fa-b78e-7cb975b192ab"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Added elements are highlighted in green, deleted elements are highlighted in red, and modified or moved elements are highlighted in blue.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reviewing_local_changes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>If you want to explore the changes of a certain element in more detail, select an element, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_diff.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the right side of the <strong>Local Changes</strong> tab, then select one of the following options:<ul><li><strong>Quick Diff</strong> - <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">to quickly inspect the changes of element property values (such as documentation, type, or multiplicity).</span></li><li><strong>Full Diff</strong> - <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">to inspect the changes made to diagrams or element property values in </span>more detail,<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>as well as look at the properties of new relationships and directly related elements (such as new or modified attributes and operations).</span></li><li><strong>Compare projects</strong> - to compare local project changes to the latest project version on the server. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Analyzing comparison results" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to analyze comparison results]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Commit</strong> button.</li></ol><h3>Disabling commit queues</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">If putting project commits in a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) line does not work for your organization, your system administrator can<span> </span></span>disable the commit queue mechanism<span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);"><span> </span>for a particular Teamwork Cloud server.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">To disable commit queues</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open for editing the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf</em> file.</li><li>Add the following line to the file:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30b9d2ac-aec2-45c9-b57a-4de7fe17fe23"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[esi.persistence.queuecommit.enable = false]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Restart the Teamwork Cloud server.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003443 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00017: (2026x Refresh1) Configure used projects

- page_id: `304003443`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003443/2026x+Refresh1+Configure+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:30.028+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see all used projects and their versions In the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog.

To open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog

1. Login to the Teamwork Cloud server.
2. Open your server project.
3. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages . The Project Usages dialog opens.
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The ****Advanced Project Usages Configuration**** dialog opens. Search barYou can use the search bar to faster filter used projects by name. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this dialog you can make various modifications for one or more projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and:

- Click the Notify on New Version check-box to get notifications when a new version of a particular project is available on the server.
- Use the commands in the toolbar or shortcut menu to:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using server projects' space=''] to the server project you are working with
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Stop using the project in your project' space=''] from the server project you are working with.
  - Start using an indirectly used project directly.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project versions' space=''] of a selected used project.
  - Review the information of a selected used project.
  - Reset changes to a selected used project. You must do this before applying selected actions!

In the **Action**column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the **Apply** button is clicked.

To turn the action on, click one of the following:

- Apply - to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. Please note, the applied changes cannot be undone.
- OK - to apply changes and close the dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can see all used projects and their versions In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Login to the Teamwork Cloud server.</li><li>Open your server project.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Collaborate </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>. The <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong><strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong></strong> dialog opens.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2f9b9a6-313b-47ff-b4ef-12b29d090646"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Search bar</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can use the search bar to faster filter used projects by name.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="advanced_project_usages_configuration_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><p>In this dialog you can make various modifications for one or more projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and:</p><ul><li>Click the <strong>Notify on New Version </strong>check-box to get notifications when a new version of a particular project is available on the server.</li><li>Use the commands in the toolbar or shortcut menu to:<ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using server projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add a new project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the server project you are working with</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Stop using the project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Remove a used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the server project you are working with.</li></ul><ul><li>Start using an indirectly used project directly.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project versions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Change the version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a selected used project.</li><li>Review the information of a selected used project.</li><li>Reset changes to a selected used project. You must do this before applying selected actions!</li></ul></li></ul><p>In the <strong>Action </strong>column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the <strong>Apply</strong> button is clicked.</p><p>To turn the action on, click one of the following:</p><ul><li><strong>Apply </strong>- to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. Please note, the applied changes cannot be undone.</li><li><strong>OK </strong>- to apply changes and close the dialog.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003770 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00018: (2026x Refresh1) Configuring used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `304003770`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003770/2026x+Refresh1+Configuring+used+projects+in+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:45.713+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > (2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages in 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see all used projects and their versions in the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog.

To open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog

1. In the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, click [ATTACHMENT filename='usages_configuration.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='advanced_project_usages_configuration.png']

In the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog, you can modify one or several used projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and use the commands in the toolbar or shortcut menu to:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using a server project in 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''] to the server project you are working with
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Removing server project usages in 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''] from the server project you are working with.
- Start using an indirectly used project directly.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project iterations' space=''] of a selected used project.
- Review the information of a selected used project.
- Reset changes to a selected used project. NOTE: You must do this before applying the selected actions!

In the **Action**column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the **Apply** button is clicked.

To turn the action on, click one of the following:

- Apply - to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. Please note that the applied changes cannot be undone.
- OK - to apply changes and close the dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can see all used projects and their versions in the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Collaborate </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Configuring used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="advanced_project_usages_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Configuring used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br />In the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog, you can modify one or several used projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and u<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ea8e0aae-4b61-415d-bd5c-322e1a1fa7e6">se the commands in the toolbar</ac:inline-comment-marker> or shortcut menu to:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using a server project in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add a new project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the server project you are working with</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Removing server project usages in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Remove a used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the server project you are working with.</li><li>Start using an indirectly used project directly.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project iterations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Change the version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a selected used project.</li><li>Review the information of a selected used project.</li><li>Reset changes to a selected used project. NOTE: You must do this before applying the selected actions!</li></ul><p>In the <strong>Action </strong>column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the <strong>Apply</strong> button is clicked.</p><p>To turn the action on, click one of the following:</p><ul><li><strong>Apply </strong>- to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. Please note that the applied changes cannot be undone.</li><li><strong>OK </strong>- to apply changes and close the dialog.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003914 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00019: (2026x Refresh1) Creating and managing categories

- page_id: `304003914`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003914/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+and+managing+categories
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:55.583+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using categories and grouping projects into them, you can organize the structure of your server projects' repository according to your needs.

By default, there is a single category *Uncategorized*, but you can create as many categories as you need. A project can be created directly in a selected category and later easily moved to another one. The category can be created in advance or while [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space='']. Categories can be renamed and removed. Also, they can have sub-categories.

The TWCloud project categories in the modeling tool dialogs will be hidden if a user does not have the *Manage Categories* or*Read-Only*permission to at least one project inside of these categories.

Read the following to learn how

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

Categories can also be managed via the Resource application. Learn more about this in .

You need to have the Manage categories permission assigned to the Global scope to be able to create categories.

createTo create a category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Be sure the Categorized View is turned on in the dialog. If it is not, click [ATTACHMENT filename='categorized_view.png'] on the toolbar of the dialog.
3. Choose one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click anywhere in the project list area and then select [ATTACHMENT filename='create_category.png'] Create Category .
4. After the **Create Category** dialog opens, select a **Parent** category and type a new category name. Select an existing category as a **Parent** category to nest a new category within.Select a *Root* node as a **Parent** category to create a new top-level category. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click OK to confirm the creation. The category is created. You can find it in the project list ordered alphabetically.

renameTo rename a category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialogs opens.
2. Select a category or a project that belongs to the category you want to rename and then do either:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='rename_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click the selection and then select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **Rename Category**. The *Uncategorized* category cannot be renamed*.*
3. When the Rename Category dialog opens, type a new category name.
4. Click OK . The category is renamed.

moveTo move a project to another category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialogs opens.
2. Select the project you want to move and do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='move_to_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click the selection and then select [ATTACHMENT filename='move_to_category.png'] Move to Category .
3. When the**Move to Category** dialog opens, select the category to which you want to move the project.
  - To create a new category, select the Create Category button in the Move to Category dialog: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click OK . The project appears in the selected category.

- You need to have the Manage Resources permission to delete the selected category.
- You need to have the Read Resources/List Resources/List All Resources and Remove Resources permissions to delete all the resources within the selected category.

removeTo remove the category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialogs opens.
2. Select the category or the project that belongs to the category you want to remove and then do either:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click the selection and then select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **Remove Category**. The *Uncategorized* category cannot be removed*.*
3. Click Yes to confirm your decision. The category is removed from the server repository together with all the resources in it .

Empty categories are by default shown in the **Manage Projects** and **Open Server Project** dialogs, but you can simply hide them.

emptyTo hide empty categories

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='show_empty_categories.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog. Click the button once more to show the empty categories again.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Using categories and grouping projects into them, you can organize the structure of your server projects' repository according to your <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0451c6b3-39ec-4d32-8c98-6f3a74b67cbe">needs</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p>By default, there is a single category <em>Uncategorized</em>, but you can create as many categories as you need. A project can be created directly in a selected category and later easily moved to another one. The category can be created in advance or while <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[adding the project to the server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Categories can be renamed and removed. Also, they can have sub-categories. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ebd8ec1-5ec1-4393-aa4e-8c6e1015f947"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The TWCloud project categories in the modeling tool dialogs will be hidden if a user does not have the <em>Manage Categories</em> o</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">r </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Read-Only</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> permission to at least one project inside of these categories.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Read the following to learn how</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="create"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To create a single category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="rename"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To rename a category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="move"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To move a project to another category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="remove"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To remove the category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To hide empty categories]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edc918a1-2b41-4d4d-9063-2ec9df702777"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Categories can also be managed via the Resource application. Learn more about this in <ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="MCS" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud User Guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ff185c0-7342-4825-a23a-92d0fc860495"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to have the Manage categories permission assigned to the Global scope to be able to create categories.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89343c45-2424-4020-a677-9d1171c262b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Be sure the Categorized View is turned on in the dialog. If it is not, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="categorized_view.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the dialog.</li><li>Choose one of the following:<br /><br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li>Right-click anywhere in the project list area and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong>Create </strong><strong>Category</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">After the <strong>Create Category</strong> dialog opens, select a <strong>Parent</strong> category and type a new category name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae28100a-bc48-428c-9d9f-3fdea3dc828a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Select an existing category as a <strong>Parent</strong> category to nest a new category within.</li><li>Select a <em>Root</em> node as a <strong>Parent</strong> category to create a new top-level category. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="root_node_nested_categories_TWC.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating and managing categories" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to confirm the creation.<br />The category is created. You can find it in the project list ordered alphabetically.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1c47db4-7792-4b2e-ba26-9b08ad30d3b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rename</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To rename a category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialogs opens.</li><li>Select a category or a project that belongs to the category you want to rename and then do either:<br /><br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li><p>Right-click the selection and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Rename Category</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e2a3933-639d-4309-adb3-bdf3a1c06c89"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <em>Uncategorized</em> category cannot be renamed<em>.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>When the <strong>Rename Category</strong> dialog opens, type a new category name.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The category is renamed.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3282daa0-3f5e-413b-b9f6-fd60f87c6991"><ac:parameter ac:name="">move</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To move a project to another category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialogs opens.</li><li>Select the project you want to move and do one of the following:<br /><br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_to_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li>Right-click the selection and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_to_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Move to Category</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p>When the<strong> Move to Category</strong> dialog opens, select the category to which you want to move the project.</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a new category, select the</span><strong> Create Category</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> button in the </span><strong>Move to Category</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> dialog:</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_category_button_in_move_to_category_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating and managing categories" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The project appears in the selected category.</li></ol><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0d68d02-ea3c-49ac-95f7-87c9b7177417"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You need to have the Manage Resources permission to delete the selected category.</li><li>You need to have the Read Resources/List Resources/List All Resources and Remove Resources permissions to delete all the resources within the selected category.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05ffedaf-49fc-4840-855d-647482274ec4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To remove the category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialogs opens.</li><li>Select the category or the project that belongs to the category you want to remove and then do either:<ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li><p>Right-click the selection and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Remove Category</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="938889ef-613c-4d9a-9dc7-85382930ad60"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <em>Uncategorized</em> category cannot be removed<em>.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Yes</strong> to confirm your decision.<br />The category is removed from the server repository <strong>together with all the resources in it</strong>. </li></ol><p> </p><p>Empty categories are by default shown in the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> and <strong>Open Server Project</strong> dialogs, but you can simply hide them.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab80dfe7-3d1a-4112-93d1-9abad07af7f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To hide empty categories</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_empty_categories.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog. Click the button once more to show the empty categories again.<br /><br /></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003665 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00020: (2026x Refresh1) Creating revisions and branches

- page_id: `304003665`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003665/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+revisions+and+branches
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:39.330+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page:**

3

Project branching enables multiple users to work on the same model at the same time. To do this, you need to create a project branch which is a copy of the project. You can modify the branch as required, independently of other branches. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model merge in Teamwork Cloud' space=''] back together when required. Conflicts between the branches are resolved during the merge process.

##### Understanding project branching

To understand how project branching works, you should be familiar with the following terms:

- Iteration - the smallest possible milestone of the project development. In other words, it is a project version you can open and review created after [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']committing the project to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform . Iterations are labeled by numbers in ascending order starting from 0 (zero).
- Revision - a larger project development milestone consisting of one or several iterations. Revisions are labeled by letters from A to Z. Note that iterations of different revisions within a branch are numbered independently as shown below.
- Branch - a copy of a project made at a certain point of the project development. Branches are labeled by numbers in ascending order starting from 1 (branch 1 is the root project). Each project branch can be developed independently and later merged with another branch. Every project branch consists of one or several revisions. Revisions of different project branches are labeled independently as shown below.

#### TIP: Iteration labeling

Iteration labeling

[branch name if specified] branch.revision (iteration)

3.B (4)

Mass Analysis

[Mass Analysis] 3.B (4)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project branching in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

##### Creating revisions and branches

You can create new branches and revisions from any revision of any branch of a project. There is no limit to the number of branches and revisions you can create.

To create a project revision or branch

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Collaborate > Open Server Project .
  - In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Collaborate > Projects .
2. In the open dialog, click [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_arrow.png'] on the tile of the project in which you want to create a revision or branch and select Edit Branches . [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_branches_command.png']
3. In the **Edit Branches** dialog, do one of the following:
  - To create a revision, select a revision, click the Create Revision button. The revision is always created from the last iteration of the selected revision.
  - To create a branch, select an iteration, click the Create Branch button. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_revision_or_branch.png']
4. In the open dialog, enter an optional title and comment for a new branch or an optional comment for a new revision.
5. Click OK .

After completing the above steps, a revision or branch is created and displayed in the **Edit Branches** dialog. A created revision or the first revision of a created branch holds information about the iteration it was created from. This information is displayed between brackets next to the name of a new revision.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong> On this page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63976275-6e85-499f-98da-ce2eb4802290"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Project branching enables multiple users to work on the same model at the same time. To do this, you need to create a project branch which is a copy of the project. You can modify the branch as required, independently of other branches. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model merge in Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Branches can be merged]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> back together when required. Conflicts between the branches are resolved during the merge process.</p><h3>Understanding project branching</h3><p>To understand how project branching works, you should be familiar with the following terms:</p><ul><li><strong>Iteration</strong> - the smallest possible milestone of the project development. In other words, it is a project version you can open and review created after <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>committing the project to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link>. Iterations are labeled by numbers in ascending order starting from 0 (zero).</li><li><strong>Revision</strong> -  a larger project development milestone consisting of one or several iterations. Revisions are labeled by letters from A to Z. Note that iterations of different revisions within a branch are numbered independently as shown below.</li><li><strong>Branch</strong> - a copy of a project made at a certain point of the project development. Branches are labeled by numbers in ascending order starting from 1 (branch 1 is the root project). Each project branch can be developed independently and later merged with another branch. Every project branch consists of one or several revisions. Revisions of different project branches are labeled independently as shown below.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a97ab2d2-ad9a-4939-a972-fa5468e3c605"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Iteration labeling</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Since iterations of different project branches are not unique, a combination of a branch, revision, and iteration is used to define a certain iteration. A unique project iteration is labeled in the following format: <strong>[branch name if specified] branch.revision (iteration)</strong>. For example iteration 4 of revision B of branch 3 would be labeled as <strong>3.B (4)</strong>. If branch 3 had a name, e.g., <em>Mass Analysis</em>, the iteration would be labeled as <strong>[Mass Analysis] 3.B (4)</strong>. You can see the iteration of an open project in the header of the modeling tool window next to the project name.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="branching.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating revisions and branches" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Project branching in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</h6><h3>Creating revisions and branches</h3><p>You can create new branches and revisions from any revision of any branch of a project. There is no limit to the number of branches and revisions you can create.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a project revision or branch</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, select<span> </span><strong>Collaborate</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Open Server Project</strong>.</li><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, select<span> </span><strong>Collaborate</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Projects</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the open dialog, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_arrow.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating revisions and branches" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the tile of the project in which you want to create a revision or branch and select <strong>Edit Branches</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_branches_command.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating revisions and branches" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Edit Branches</strong> dialog, do one of the following:</p><ul><li>To create a revision, select a revision, click the <strong>Create Revision</strong> button. The revision is always created from the last iteration of the selected revision.</li><li>To create a branch, select an iteration, click the <strong>Create Branch</strong> button.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_revision_or_branch.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating revisions and branches" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>In the open dialog, enter an optional title and comment for a new branch or an optional comment for a new revision.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><br /></li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps, a revision or branch is created and displayed in the <strong>Edit Branches</strong> dialog. A created revision or the first revision of a created branch holds information about the iteration it was created from. This information is displayed between brackets next to the name of a new revision.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003607 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00021: (2026x Refresh1) Disconnected team collaboration

- page_id: `304003607`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003607/2026x+Refresh1+Disconnected+team+collaboration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:36.266+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can collaborate with people working on different Teamwork Cloud and **3D**EXPERIENCE platform instances by using the server project file format (.szip). When a project is exported to a server project file, you can use it to update an existing project or import it as a new project on another server. Server project files can be used in the following way:

- To import projects from one Teamwork Cloud server to another Teamwork Cloud server.
- To import projects from the Teamwork Cloud server to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform.
- To import projects from one 3D EXPERIENCE platform to another 3D EXPERIENCE platform.

You cannot open server project files locally. To view the contents of a server project file, you must import it to the server as a new project or use it to update an existing project.

For more information, see the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can collaborate with people working on different Teamwork Cloud and <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform instances by using the server project file format (.szip). When a project is exported to a server project file, you can use it to update an existing project or import it as a new project on another server. Server project files can be used in the following way:</p><ul><li>To import projects from one Teamwork Cloud server to another Teamwork Cloud server.</li><li>To import projects from the Teamwork Cloud server to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</li><li>To import projects from one <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform to another <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bbff92c-4d1e-40a0-9efe-709b8046ce9f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You cannot open server project files locally. To view the contents of a server project file, you must import it to the server as a new project or use it to update an existing project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For more information, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f99710af-594e-43f5-8d82-e2f2aeae2c77" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003971 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00022: (2026x Refresh1) Document templates

- page_id: `304003971`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003971/2026x+Refresh1+Document+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:12:06.050+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > (2026x Refresh1) Using Cameo Collaborator > (2026x Refresh1) Publishing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When configuring settings for publishing a model to Cameo Collaborator, you must choose a template to determine the appearance and content of a published model. There are two types of templates you can use:

- **Predefined templates** - These templates are stored in the*modelLibraries*folder of the modeling tool's installation directory. They appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog if they are used in the project you are going to publish. Be aware that the list of available templates depends on your modeling tool, e.g., MagicDraw (with or without plugins), Cameo Systems Modeler, etc.
- **Custom templates** - These templates are .mdzip files created or modified by a user and stored in the user-defined location. They appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog if they are stored or used in the project you are going to publish. [Learn how to create custom Cameo Collaborator templates](https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&viewType=document§ionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&guest=true).

The following table describes all predefined Cameo Collaborator document templates and lists the modeling tools where they are available.

| Template name | Short description | Available in... |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BPMN Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a model as the process portal, which provides the following views of the model:ProcessesData ObjectsOrganization StructuresInformation Systems | Cameo Enterprise Architecture / Magic Systems of Systems ArchitectCameo Business Modeler Plugin |
| Entire Model | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal, which provides a single view of the entire model. | MagicDraw / Magic Software ArchitectCameo Systems Modeler / Magic Cyber Systems EngineerCameo Enterprise Architecture / Magic Systems of Systems Architect |
| Diagrams View Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal providing the diagrams view of the model. |  |
| Requirements Document Template | Apply this template to publish a project as a single-view Requirements Report portal. | MagicDraw / Magic Software ArchitectCameo Systems Modeler/ Magic Cyber Systems EngineerCameo Enterprise Architecture/ Magic Systems of Systems ArchitectCameo Requirements Modeler PluginSysML v1 PluginUPDM 2 Plugin |
| Requirements Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a project as the Requirements portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureTest CasesTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams |  |
| Software Engineering Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a project as the Software Engineering portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureImplementationTest CasesArchitecture TraceabilityTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams | MagicDraw / Magic Software ArchitectCameo Systems Modeler / Magic Cyber Systems EngineerCameo Enterprise Architecture / Magic Systems of Systems Architect |
| SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a project as the SysML v1 portal, which provides the following views of the model:RequirementsStructureInterfacesConstraintsBehaviorDiagrams | MagicDraw / Magic Software ArchitectCameo Systems Modeler/ Magic Cyber Systems EngineerCameo Enterprise Architecture/ Magic Systems of Systems ArchitectSysML v1 PluginUPDM 2 Plugin |
| UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:50%Security ViewResources View - ResourcesSystems View - ResourcesPhysical Resources View - ResourcesResources View - Resource ExchangesSystems View - Resource ExchangesPhysical Resource View - Resource ExchangesResources View - FunctionsSystems View - FunctionsPhysical Resources View - FunctionsService Oriented View - Service InterfacesServices View - Service InterfacesService Oriented View - Service SpecificationsServices View - Service SpecificationsService Oriented View - Service FunctionsServices View - Service FunctionsProgramme View - ProjectsProject View - ProjectsAcquisition View - Projects50%Personnel View - Organisational RelationshipsOperational View - Organizational RelationshipsLogical View - Organisational RelationshipsLogical View - Operational ExchangesOperational View - Operational ExchangesLogical View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational PerformersLogical View - Operational PerformersStrategic View - Enterprise PhasesCapability View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - CapabilitiesCapability View - CapabilitiesStrategic View - CapabilitiesDiagramsData and InformationDictionaryTechnical Standards | Cameo Enterprise Architecture/ Magic Systems of Systems ArchitectUAF Plugin |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When configuring settings for publishing a model to Cameo Collaborator, you must choose a template to determine the appearance and content of a published model. There are two types of templates you can use:</p><ul><li><p><strong>Predefined templates</strong> - These templates are stored in <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">the </span><em>modelLibraries</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> folder of the modeling tool's installation directory</span>. They appear on the list of the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog if they are used in the project you are going to publish. Be aware that the list of available templates depends on your modeling tool, e.g., MagicDraw (with or without plugins), Cameo Systems Modeler, etc.</p></li><li><p><strong>Custom templates</strong> - These templates are .mdzip files created or modified by a user and stored in the user-defined location. They appear on the list of the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog if they are stored or used in the project you are going to publish. <a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">Learn how to create custom Cameo Collaborator templates</a>.</p></li></ul><p>The following table describes all predefined Cameo Collaborator document templates and lists the modeling tools where they are available.</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 86.4509%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 19.805%;" /><col class="" style="width: 50.733%;" /><col class="" style="width: 29.462%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Template name</th><th>Short description</th><th>Available in...</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>BPMN Template for Cameo Collaborator</strong></td><td><p>Apply this template to publish a model as the process portal, which provides the following views of the model:</p><ul><li>Processes</li><li>Data Objects</li><li>Organization Structures</li><li>Information Systems</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture / Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li><li>Cameo Business Modeler Plugin</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Entire Model</strong></td><td><p>Apply this template to publish a model as the portal, which provides a single view of the entire model.</p></td><td rowspan="2"><ul><li>MagicDraw / Magic Software Architect</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler / Magic Cyber Systems Engineer</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture / Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li></ul><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Diagrams View Template for Cameo Collaborator</strong></td><td>Apply this template to publish a model as the portal providing the diagrams view of the model.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Requirements Document Template</strong></td><td>Apply this template to publish a project as a single-view Requirements Report portal.</td><td rowspan="2"><ul><li>MagicDraw / Magic Software Architect</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler/ Magic Cyber Systems Engineer</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture/ Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li><li>Cameo Requirements Modeler Plugin</li><li>SysML v1 Plugin</li><li>UPDM 2 Plugin</li></ul><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Requirements Template for Cameo <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003455 space=CM version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 00023: (2026x Refresh1) Editing the content of a used project

- page_id: `304003455`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003455/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+the+content+of+a+used+project
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:23:17.538+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To switch versions of the used projects you must have the[Administer Resources permission](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/?contextKey=permissions&version=2026x Refresh1&variant=default).

Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) projects can only use other projects in the read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as stand-alone.

To open a used project for editing

1. In the Model Browser, right-click the project to open a shortcut menu under the package Project Usages .
2. Click Project Usages and select:
  - **Open Latest Version** to open the latest version of the used project for editing. Note that the version used in the project does **not** change after editing the used project. Updating used project versionsIf you select the check-box **Notify on New Version** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project versions' space='']**Project Usages**dialog for a particular used project, you will be notified about the existence of a later used project version while reloading the main project.
  - Open This version to open the actual project version for editing. If the used version is not the latest one, the project will open in the Read-only mode.

Now you can modify the structure and content of the project.

#### WARNING: Updating used project version

Updating used project version

Please note, the used project version in the main project remains the same. You should [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project versions' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="660e2072-9c67-4bfd-a314-4d8ce037351b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To switch versions of the used projects you must have the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/?contextKey=permissions&amp;version=2026x Refresh1&amp;variant=default">Administer Resources permission</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) projects can onl<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0885cc63-f2d7-4520-8705-6cc0d04d1515">y u</ac:inline-comment-marker>se other projects in the read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as stand-alone.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open a used project for editing</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, right-click the project to open a shortcut menu under the package <em>Project Usages</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>Project Usages</strong> and select:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Open Latest Version</strong> to open the latest version of the used project for editing. Note that the version used in the project does <strong>not</strong> change after editing the used project. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df1e882b-86c6-4380-b341-b0f86032077f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating used project versions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you select the check-box <strong>Notify on New Version</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project versions" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> for a particular used project, you will be notified about the existence of a later used project version <span>while reloading the main project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><strong>Open This version</strong> to open the actual project version for editing. If the used version is not the latest one, the project will open in the Read-only mode.</li></ul></li></ol><p>Now you can modify the structure and content of the project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ecc64b9-4974-4f09-ab53-3e90c70944ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating used project version</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please note, the used project version in the main project remains the same. You should <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing used project versions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update the usage manually]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003620 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00025: (2026x Refresh1) Enabling 3DEXPERIENCE collaboration

- page_id: `304003620`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003620/2026x+Refresh1+Enabling+3DEXPERIENCE+collaboration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:36.961+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To start collaborating on projects in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you must enable the collaboration environment powered by the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform in your modeling tool.

If you launch your modeling tool through the platform, these steps are not necessary because you will be automatically logged in to the platform.

To enable collaboration powered by the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, select Options > Environment .
2. On the left side of the Environment Options dialog, select the Collaboration option group.
3. In the option specification pane, set the Enable Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform option to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='collaboration_in_environment_options.png']
4. Click OK to close the Environment Options dialog.
5. Click OK in the open message dialog.
6. Restart the modeling tool to apply changes.

After enabling the collaboration environment, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform and start a collaboration session.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To start collaborating on projects in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7ec893ba-977d-4ca6-946b-10f51b726632"><strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE</ac:inline-comment-marker> platform, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7d1d0d4c-e1ae-4456-a31e-32ff39e8cdaf">you must enable</ac:inline-comment-marker> the collaboration environment powered by the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform in your modeling tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a37167b5-86fb-4633-8209-9aa1dd83d319"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you launch your modeling tool through the platform, these steps are not necessary because you will be automatically logged in to the platform.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To enable collaboration powered by the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Collaboration</strong> option group.</li><li>In the option specification pane, set the <strong>Enable Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform</strong> option to <em>true</em>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_in_environment_options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Enabling 3DEXPERIENCE collaboration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> in the open message dialog.</li><li>Restart the modeling tool to apply changes.</li></ol><p><br />After enabling the collaboration environment, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>log in to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link> and start a collaboration session.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003763 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00026: (2026x Refresh1) Exporting a package to a new 3DEXPERIENCE server project

- page_id: `304003763`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003763/2026x+Refresh1+Exporting+a+package+to+a+new+3DEXPERIENCE+server+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:45.244+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > (2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages in 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Exporting a package to a separate project is the first step of model decomposition. Then you can[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using a server project in 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''] that project in other projects. You can export a package to a new server project if you need to detach a part of the model from the project. The part can be a library or a fragment of the model simultaneously developed by another team. You can use the part of the model you moved in *more than one* project.

To export a package to a new **3D**EXPERIENCE server project

1. In the Model Browser, right-click the package to export.
2. Select Project Usages > Export Packages to New Server Project . The Export Packages to New Project dialog opens. The package is already selected in this dialog.
3. Click OK .
4. If there are any cyclic dependencies, they are shown in the **Package Dependencies** panel : [ATTACHMENT filename='package_dependencies.png'] You cannot export the package if it has cyclic dependencies. Eliminate the cyclic dependencies before trying to export the package again.
5. If no cyclic dependencies are detected, you see a dialog with the options to change the package name and/or add a commit comment. Make any necessary changes and click OK.
6. Wait for package export to finish.

If packages being exported from the source project to the target project are dependent on other elements that are NOT exported together, the necessary usages to used projects are created in the project these packages are moved to. This is being done with the intention of keeping all the dependencies valid.

The newly created server project has the structure and the contents of the exported package and is automatically used in the project from which it was detached. The used project appears under the package *Project Usages* in the Model Browser, and the exported package no longer appears under the root package in the Model Browser.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using server projects' space=''] the newly created server project in other projects.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
