````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Exporting a package to a separate project is the first step of model decomposition. Then you can </span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="be32ff2e-d034-4d44-9f5c-1007d3a26682"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using a server project in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reuse]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that project in other projects. You can e</ac:inline-comment-marker>xport a package to a new server project if you need to detach a part of the model from the project. The part can be a library or a fragment of the model simultaneously developed by another team. You can use the part of the model you moved in <em>more than one</em> project.</p><p>To export a package to a new <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE server project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, right-click the package to export.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Export Packages to New Server Project</strong>. The <strong>Export Packages to New Project</strong> dialog opens. The package is already selected in this dialog.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>If there are any cyclic dependencies, they are shown in the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Package Dependencies</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> panel</span></span>:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_dependencies.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><u>You cannot export the package if it has cyclic dependencies.</u> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2a00a888-a41d-4faa-a604-6568215da5e2">Eliminate the cyclic dependencies before trying to export the package again.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li><li>If no cyclic dependencies are detected, you see a dialog with the options to change the package name and/or add a commit comment. Make any necessary changes and click <strong>OK.</strong></li><li>Wait for package export to finish.<br /><strong> </strong></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adad3d55-5d95-4bac-836b-0e6fe375f07c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If packages being exported from the source project to the target project are dependent on other elements that are NOT exported together, the necessary usages to used projects are created in the project these packages are moved to. This is being done with the intention of keeping all the dependencies valid.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The newly created server project has the structure and the contents of the exported package and is automatically used in the project from which it was detached. The used project appears under the package <em>Project Usages</em> in the Model Browser, and the exported package no longer appears under the root package in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="443c6de6-a6b9-4900-b26c-4db8287e9c4e"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="451920f1-a81f-432c-b351-7090b8391e58">Model Browser</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_export.png" /></ac:image></p><p>You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using server projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reuse]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the newly created server project in other projects.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003420 space=CM version=2 -->
## PAGE 00027: (2026x Refresh1) Exporting a package to a new server project

- page_id: `304003420`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003420/2026x+Refresh1+Exporting+a+package+to+a+new+server+project
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:23:17.281+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### WARNING: Permissions

Permissions

To export packages to a new server project, you must have:

- Read Resources, Edit Resources, Edit Resource Properties, and Administer Resources [permissions](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/?contextKey=permissions&version=2026x Refresh1&variant=default) on the main project
- Create Resources permission for the selected category a new server project will be stored in

Exporting a package to a separate project is the first step of the model decomposition.It is followed by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using server projects' space=''] that project in other projects.Exporting a package to a new server project is necessary when you need to detach a part of the model from the project. The part can be a library or a fragment of the model simultaneously developed by another team. You can use the part of the model you moved in *more than one* project.

To export a package to a new server project

1. In the Model Browser, right-click the package to export.
2. Select Project Usages > Export Packages to New Server Project . The Export Packages to New Project dialog opens. The package is already selected in this dialog.
3. Click OK .
4. When the message asking to show used read-only projects appears, click Yes (this is our recommendation).
5. When the message asking to check for dependencies between the package and the rest of the model appears, click Yes (this is our recommendation). If the package depends on other projects, eliminate those dependencies before exporting it.
6. In the Project Name dialog, do the following:
  1. Change the default name of the new server project, if you choose.
  2. Select a category to store the new project.
  3. Add a comment for the project.
  4. Click OK .
7. Wait while the export completes.

If packages being exported from the source project to the target project are dependent on other elements that are NOT exported together, the necessary usages to used projects are created in the project these packages are moved to. This is being done with the intention of keeping all the dependencies valid.

The newly created server project has the structure and the contents of the exported package and is automatically used in the project from which it was detached. The used project appears under the package *Project Usages* in the Model Browser, and the exported package no longer appears under the root package in the Model Browser.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using server projects' space=''] the newly created server project in other projects.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The part can be a library or a fragment of the model simultaneously developed by another team. You can use the part of the model you moved in <em>more than one</em> project.</p><p><br /></p><p>To export a package to a new server project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, right-click the package to export.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Export Packages to New Server Project</strong>. The <strong>Export Packages to New Project</strong> dialog opens. The package is already selected in this dialog.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>When the message asking to show used read-only projects appears, click <strong>Yes</strong> (this is our recommendation).</li><li>When the message asking to check for dependencies between the package and the rest of the model appears, click <strong>Yes</strong> (this is our recommendation). If the package depends on other projects, eliminate those dependencies before exporting it.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Name</strong> dialog, do the following:<br /><ol><li>Change the default name of the new server project, if you choose.</li><li>Select a category to store the new project.</li><li>Add a comment for the project.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>Wait while the export completes.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adad3d55-5d95-4bac-836b-0e6fe375f07c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If packages being exported from the source project to the target project are dependent on other elements that are NOT exported together, the necessary usages to used projects are created in the project these packages are moved to. This is being done with the intention of keeping all the dependencies valid.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The newly created server project has the structure and the contents of the exported package and is automatically used in the project from which it was detached. The used project appears under the package <em>Project Usages</em> in the Model Browser, and the exported package no longer appears under the root package in the Model Browser.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Model Browser before and after the package export" ac:alt="Model Browser before and after the package export"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_browser_before_after_export.png" /></ac:image></p><p>You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using server projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reuse]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the newly created server project in other projects.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003712 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00028: (2026x Refresh1) Exporting projects to server project files

- page_id: `304003712`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003712/2026x+Refresh1+Exporting+projects+to+server+project+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:42.092+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > (2026x Refresh1) Disconnected team collaboration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export a project to a server project file (.szip) and thenuse the file to update an existing project or import it as a new project in another **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

To export a project to a server project file

1. In the main menu, select File > Export To > Server Project File > With Used Projects .
2. In the open dialog, select the file location and specify its name.
3. Click the Save button.

The server project file is saved in the selected location. For instructions on how to import the file to another server, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing server project files to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can export a project to a server project file (.szip) and then<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>use the file to update an existing project or import it as a new project in a<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">nother <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</span></span></span></p><p><br /></p><p>To export a project to a server project file</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Export To</strong> &gt; <strong>Server Project File</strong> &gt; <strong>With Used Projects</strong>.</li><li>In the open dialog, select the file location and specify its name.</li><li>Click the <strong>Save</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />The server project file is saved in the selected location. For instructions on how to import the file to another server, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing server project files to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003788 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00029: (2026x Refresh1) FAQ about used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `304003788`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003788/2026x+Refresh1+FAQ+about+used+projects+in+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:47.080+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > (2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages in 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Can I import a used project?**

The **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform does not support importing used projects.

**Can I remove a used project but keep the references?**

The **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform doesnot support recovered elements. Thus, you cannot remove used projects while keeping the references. You only have the option to remove a used project together with its references, as shown below:

****

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Removing a used project.

**I cannot open a project that uses other projects and see a message that a used project is missing. Why?**If you do not have access to a used project or it is deleted, you cannot open the main project. You must have access to all content that is used in the main project.

**Why can I not see the option to export packages to a new project?**

The **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform does not support exporting packages to a new project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Thus, you cannot remove used projects while keeping the references. Y<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">ou only have the option to remove a used project together with its references, as shown below:</span></p><p><strong> </strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing_used_project.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FAQ about used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Removing a used project.</h6><p><strong>I cannot open a project that uses other projects and see a message that a used project is missing. Why?<br /></strong>If you do not have access to a used project or it is deleted, you cannot open the main project. You must have access to all content that is used in the main project.</p><p><strong>Why can I not see the option to export packages to a new project?</strong></p><p>The <span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"><strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE </span>platform does not support exporting packages to a new project.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003579 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00030: (2026x Refresh1) Global element usage search

- page_id: `304003579`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003579/2026x+Refresh1+Global+element+usage+search
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:34.865+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The global element usage search allows you to check the usages of elements within the Teamwork Cloud repository. This makes it easier to examine how deleting or modifying an element can impact the projects that use it. The following demo shows how this functionality works:

600400

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- You can only use the Global Element Usage Search feature if [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space='MCS'] on the server.
- You can investigate element usages only in projects that have been [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating indexing configurations' space='MCS'].
- If you use a server of an earlier version than your modeling tool, you may need to specify the Web Application Platform URL option in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MT']**Environment Options** dialog .

The command is only available for the elements in an open (main) project. You cannot execute it if:

- the selected element is from a Used Project
- the selected element has not yet been committed to the server
- multiple elements are selected

To find element usages

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MT'] or diagram pane, right-click an element and select Related Elements > Search in Server > Used by in Server . Choose this option if you want to search solely for the usages of the element.
  - In the Containment tab or diagram pane, right-click an element and select Related Elements > Search in Server > Used by in Server Recursively . Choose this option if you want to search for the usages of both the element and all of the elements that are owned by it.

The element usage search results panel opens with a list of server projects that use the selected element:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Only the latest indexed branch versions are found. For example, if an element is used in the historical version but is no longer in use, it is not displayed in the element usage search results panel.

If you see a warning message (see below), it means that you do not have permission to read the project(s) using the element you performed the usage search for.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Press the Refresh button [IMAGE alt='' src='']to see the latest information on element usage.

2. Select a project to see a list of project branches and versions thatuse the element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Press **Actions [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > Open project**to open the specific project branch that uses the element.

3. To investigate element usage in more detail, select a specific branch. This shows usage details at the element level:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space='MCS']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating indexing configurations' space='MCS']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The global element usage search allows you to check the usages of elements within the Teamwork Cloud repository. This makes it easier to examine how deleting or modifying an element can impact the projects that use it. The following demo shows how this functionality works:<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30b067a8-677a-493c-96e3-f83d1ee3d132"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1TUWPUtM4Pc" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a596da95-c8bd-4ec6-932d-e8132d0bcfc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can only use the Global Element Usage Search feature if <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Elasticsearch is installed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the server.</li><li><p>You can investigate element usages only in projects that have been <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating indexing configurations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[indexed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li>If you use a server of an earlier version than your modeling tool, you may need to specify the <strong>Web Application Platform URL</strong> option in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The command is only available for the elements in an open (main) project. You cannot execute it if:</p><ul><li>the selected element is from a Used Project</li><li>the selected element has not yet been committed to the server</li><li>multiple elements are selected</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To find element usages</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ac:link><span> </span>or diagram pane, right-click an element and select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt;<span> </span><strong>Search in Server &gt; Used by in Server</strong>. Choose this option if you want to search solely for the usages of the element.</li><li>In the <a class="external-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Containment+tab">Containment tab</a><span> </span>or diagram pane, right-click an element and select <strong>Related Elements</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Search in Server &gt; Used by in Server Recursively</strong>. Choose this option if you want to search for the usages of <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">both the element and </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">all of the elements that are owned by it.</span></li></ul></li></ol><p>The element usage search results panel opens with a list of server projects that use the selected element:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="global_element_usage_search_results.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c567ba2-6e9a-436f-83f3-77a6c65a5ea1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only the latest indexed branch versions are found. For example, if an element is used in the historical version but is no longer in use, it is not displayed in the element usage search results panel.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you see a warning message (see below), it means that you do not have permission to read the project(s) using the element you performed the usage search for.</p><p><ac:image ac:width="674"><ri:attachment ri:filename="some_usages_cannot_be_displayed.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b21078d-3a35-432c-b9ec-f8b2f0be9ee2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Press the Refresh button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>t<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">o see the latest information on element usage</span><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>2. Select a project to see a list of project <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">branches and versions that </span>use the element.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_usage_in_project_branches.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23ec14e1-8db2-4969-a4f4-4b855a177c91"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Press <strong>Actions <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actions_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">&gt; Open project</span> </strong>to open the specific project branch that uses the element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>3. To investigate element usage in more detail, select a specific branch. This shows usage details at the element level:</p><p><ac:image ac:width="674"><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_usage_details.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Global element usage search" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating indexing configurations" /></ac:link></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003482 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00031: (2026x Refresh1) Hiding packages

- page_id: `304003482`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003482/2026x+Refresh1+Hiding+packages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:30.957+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['hidden-packages', 'non-hidden-part', 'non-hidden-model', 'non-hidden-project', 'synchronization', 'manual-synchronization', 'automatic-synchronization', 'stripping-project', 'reusing-model', 'reusing-project']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

1191527483

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

1191527485

INLINE

1333798886

INLINE

**On this page**

33

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

1191527484

INLINE

The Hiding packages utility lets you specify which model[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MED']elements within the model packages, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages' space=''] should be hidden. Once specified, the public version of a model will be created by cutting/deleting the hidden parts. You can strip your project to reuse only a particular part of the project in another project. To strip your project, you must hide packages you do not want to share.

700500

##### Hiding packages

To hide packages

1. Open a server project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click the package you want to hide. On the shortcut menu, click Project Usages > Hide Packages .
  - On the main menu, click Collaborate > Hidden packages .
3. The **Hidden Packages** dialog opens. In the **Tree/List**select the packages you want to hide and****add them to the **Hidden Packages**side. Move the selected package back to the **Tree/List**side to unhide it. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click **OK** when you are done.
5. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space='MT'] is suggested. Usage checking may be time-consuming; however, it helps to identify elements that are related to the soon-to-be hidden parts . If any issues are found, they are displayed and described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usages and dependencies search results window' space='MT']**Package Usage window** .
6. After you complete or skip Usage checking, you will see a notification that hiding the selected elements will be completed only after committing your project.
7. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud' space=''] the project.
8. The stripped model version is created to be available to use in other projects. Hidden packages are marked with a lock sign [ATTACHMENT filename='hidden_package_notation.png'] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MT'] .

##### Hiding and unhiding used projects

You can hide/unhide a used project the same way as other packages. When hiding a used project, you cannot hide separate used project parts: you need to hide the whole used project.After hiding is done, a dedicated branch is created with the stripped model version, whereall the elements of the used project are hidden from the Containment tree due to being unused, and all references to these elements are cut from other model places.

To hide an indirectly used project, you need to hide it in the directly used project first.

#### TIP: Example

Example

Let's say you have a project A that uses a project B, and this project uses a project C. You want to hide the project C inside the project A. In this case, you need to hide it in the project B directly first and then use the public branch of the project B.

Indirectly used projects can also be hidden/unhidden automatically.Typically, this happens when the status of the used project changes:

- when the used project is hidden/unhidden
- when the used project is updated
- when the used project is removed
- when the new used project is used
- when used or using projects are merged
- when the used project is exported
- when the used project is imported

To hide an indirectly used project automatically, you need to hide all directly used projects that use the specific indirectly used project.

#### TIP: Example

Example

Let's say you have a project A that uses a project B, and this project uses a project C. You hide the project B and then the project C is hidden as well; however, if you add a new usage D that also uses C, the hidden usage of the project C is automatically unhidden because not all directly used projects using the project C are hidden in this case.

##### Dedicated branch for stripped model

A stripped model is stored in a dedicated branch created when element hiding is performed for the first time. By default, this branch is named *Public Project*. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects' space=''] if you need to.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project with it`s Public Project branch. The public project now can be reused in another project.

If all packages from the model are unhidden, the dedicated branch will be deactivated.

If the main project is using a public branch of another project and that branch gets deactivated, you will be informed about this change with a warning icon in the main project with a warning icon [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in:

- Containment tree (under Project Usages root),
- Project Usages dialog,
- Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog.

The state of the used project is checked at the same time interval as checking for a new project version in the background, or by clicking the Project Usages Update button.

##### Hidden package changes

After a package or a used project is successfully hidden or unhidden, an icon with a lock on a package (or used project) is shown in a Difference viewer.

The icon with a lock on a package (or on a used project package) is also shown in:

- Upcoming Changes browser panel,
- Local changes browser panel,
- Commit dialog,
- Content History panel,
- Element History.

##### Model synchronization

You must have the read-write permissionfor the stripped model branchto synchronize it.

A stripped model is read-only. It can only be used for reuse in other projects, not for editing (it can only be modified by synchronizing it – read-write permission is required for the stripped model branch). It can only be used for reuse in other projects, not for editing. However, you can edit the main project. On committing, checking is performed to see if the changes affect hidden and public parts of the model, and model synchronization is initialized. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MT']**Project Options**dialog, you can define the model synchronization mode. The default mode is **Manual**; that is, after committing project changes, you will be notified about local changes affecting a public model stored in a dedicated branch. You can then decide either to synchronize changes or not.

The server project with hidden packages (and public branches) might get out of sync with the current version of the model in the public branch after set as the latest functionality in the **History** dialog is used. Therefore, you will be asked to perform hidden package synchronization.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To select a synchronization mode

1. On the main menu, click Options > Projects . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the list of option groups, select General and then select Collaboration in the list of options.
3. For the option Synchronization Mode , specify one of the following values:

- 
  - Automatic - to synchronize changes automatically on committing the project changes.
  - Manual (default) - to synchronize changes upon your decision.
  - None - do not perform any synchronization.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting a synchronization mode to specify how changes in the main project are synchronized to a branch where a stripped model version is stored

1191527482

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud' space='']

ac:ref="ee458cf5-7fd9-42d7-ba66-5bccef748f14">You</ac:inline-comment-marker> must have the read-write permission</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> for the stripped model branch </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">to synchronize it. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A stripped model is read-only. It can only be used for reuse in other projects, not for editing (it can only be modified by synchronizing it – read-write permission is required for the stripped model branch). It can only be used for reuse in other projects, not for editing. However, you can edit the main project. In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, you can define the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5cdbe521-8cae-47be-a959-afdbfa78efcd">model</ac:inline-comment-marker> synchronization mode. The default mode is <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Manual</strong>; that is, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="eeb6f786-be7e-472d-a58a-1bf81c497ccb">after</ac:inline-comment-marker> committing project changes, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ec088f7a-de5d-4145-b477-9562b3af9a0f">you will be notified</ac:inline-comment-marker> about local changes affecting a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4e001a51-fa61-4e5a-a17a-b1caa8b3ef17">public model</ac:inline-comment-marker> stored in a dedicated branch. You can then decide either to synchronize changes or not.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f1f7b51-8f8d-4527-b1e7-67b4c991d290"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The server project with hidden packages (and public branches) might get out of sync with the current version of the model in the public branch after set as the latest functionality in the <strong>History</strong> dialog is used. Therefore, you will be asked to perform hidden package synchronization.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Hidden_packages_out_of_sync.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hiding packages" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To select a synchronization mode</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Project Options </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the list of option groups, select <strong> General</strong> and then select <strong>Collaboration </strong>in the list of options.</li><li>For the option <strong>Synchronization Mode</strong>, specify one of the following values:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Automatic</strong> - to synchronize changes automatically on committing the project changes.</li><li><strong>Manual </strong>(default) - to synchronize changes upon your decision.</li><li><strong>None</strong> - do not perform any synchronization.</li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchronization_mode.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Hiding packages" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Selecting a synchronization mode to specify how changes in the main project are synchronized to a branch where a stripped model version is stored</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53f7957a-7599-4786-9733-1999a9bbb8d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1191527482</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Branching projects" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Identifying Package Dependencies" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003963 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00032: (2026x Refresh1) Importing server project file

- page_id: `304003963`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003963/2026x+Refresh1+Importing+server+project+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:12:05.115+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration between disconnected teams > (2026x Refresh1) Using server project file format
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a project as a new server project, import the project*.szip*file.

To import a server project file

1. Select File > Import From > Server Project File.
2. Select the .szip file you want to use and click **Open**. This opens the **Import Project to the Server**dialog: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the Action column, you see the action that is performed with the project once you click Import . Possible actions include:
  - Add to Server – the project is added to the server.
    - If the action is Add to Server , you can modify the **Name on Server**column to choose a new name for the imported project.
    - If you want to skip certain projects, you can change the Add to Server action to Skip .
  - Skip – the server project is skipped.
  - Skip (Project Removed) – the project is removed. You must use Data Manager to either delete it completely from the server or restore the deleted version.
  - Skip (Project Exists) – the project already exists on the server and cannot be imported . If you still need to upload the .szip version of the project to Teamwork Cloud, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Updating a server project with local changes' space='']**Update from Local Project**functionality.
4. To choose the category for the newly added project, select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the dialog. This opens the **Select Category**dialog : 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Choose the category and click **OK**to return to the **Import Project to the Server**dialog.
6. Click **Import**.****The project will be added as a new server project.

**Please note:**

- If the project you are importing was saved together with used projects, all of the used projects will be visible in the import dialog. You can then decide if you want to include them in the import.
- If a main or used project you are importing already exists in the server, it will be skipped and not imported. You can see the results of the import action in the Notification Window: [ATTACHMENT filename='server_projects_notification.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To add a project as a new server project, import the project </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><em>.szip </em>file.</span></p><p>To import a server project file</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">File &gt; Import From &gt; Server Project File.</span></strong></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Select the .szip file you want to use and click <strong>Open</strong>. This opens the <strong>Import Project to the Server </strong>dialog:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_project_to_server.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing server project file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li>In the <strong>Action </strong>column, you see the action that is performed with the project once you click <strong>Import</strong>. Possible actions include:<ul><li><strong>Add to Server</strong> – the project is added to the server.<ul><li>If the action is <strong>Add to Server</strong>, you can <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">modify the <strong>Name on Server </strong>column to choose a new name for the imported project.</span> </li><li>If you want to skip certain projects, you can change the <strong>Add to Server </strong>action to <strong>Skip</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Skip </strong>– the server project is skipped.</li><li><strong>Skip (Project Removed)</strong> – the project is removed. You must use Data Manager to either delete it completely from the server or restore the deleted version.</li><li><strong>Skip (Project Exists)</strong> – the project already exists on the server and cannot be <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e5075efb-8b1f-459b-8bfd-e58eb1442197">imported</ac:inline-comment-marker>. If you still need to upload the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.szip version of the project to Teamwork Cloud, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Updating a server project with local changes" /><ac:link-body><strong>Update from Local Project </strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>functionality.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To choose the category for the newly added project, select <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dots_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> at the bottom of the dialog. This opens the <strong>Select Category </strong>dialog</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="461"><ri:attachment ri:filename="server_project_category_selection.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing server project file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Choose the category and click <strong>OK </strong>to return to the <strong>Import Project to the Server </strong>dialog.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Click <strong>Import</strong>.<strong> </strong>The project will be added as a new server project.<br /><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4fdaf3a5-e1fc-4b1c-aac0-0eb146562935"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Please note:</strong></p><ul><li>If the project you are importing was saved together with used projects, all of the used projects will be visible in the import dialog. You can then decide if you want to include them in the import.</li><li>If a main or used project you are importing already exists in the server, it will be skipped and not imported.<br />You can see the results of the import action in the Notification Window:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="235"><ri:attachment ri:filename="server_projects_notification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing server project file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003714 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00033: (2026x Refresh1) Importing server project files to 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `304003714`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003714/2026x+Refresh1+Importing+server+project+files+to+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:42.413+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > (2026x Refresh1) Disconnected team collaboration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Exporting projects to server project files' space=''], you can import itto the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform. You can alsouse .szip files to update existing projects on the platform.

To import/update projects from a server project file to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. In the main menu, select File > Import From > Server Project File .
2. In the open dialog, select the .szip file you want to import and click the **Open** button. The **Import Project to the Server** dialog opens. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Import Project to the Server** dialog lists the main project and its usages that will be imported to or updated on the server. The **Action** column indicated the action that will be performed on each project:**Add to Server** - The project does not exist on the server and will be added as a new project.**Update** - An earlier project version exists on the server, and the project will be updated. During the update, a new project version is created, and the project data from the server project file overwrites the project data stored on the target server. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select which project revision or branch should be updated.**Skip** - The same version of the project you're trying to import exists on the server. The existing project version on the server will be used in the project that requires it. No new data will be imported or updated.
3. Click the Import button.

When the import is complete, the **Notification Window** opens saying that the projects were imported successfully.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>After <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exporting projects to server project files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exporting a project to the server project file (.szip)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you can import it</span><span> to the </span><strong>3D</strong><span>EXPERIENCE platform. You can also<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> use .szip files to update existing projects on the platform.</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p>To import/update projects from a server project file to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">File</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> &gt; </span><strong style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Import From</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> &gt; </span><strong style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Server Project File</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">In the open dialog, select the .szip file you want to import and click the <strong>Open</strong> button. The <strong>Import Project to the Server</strong> dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_project_to_server.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing server project files to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8cc84f47-ecc2-457a-b418-452ed36aeee3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> <strong>Import Project to the Server</strong> dialog lists the main project and its usages that will be imported to or updated on the server. The <strong>Action</strong> column indicated the action that will be performed on each project:<br /></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Add to Server</strong> - The project does not exist on the server and will be added as a new project.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Update</strong> - An earlier project version exists on the server, and the project will be updated. During the update, a new project version is created, and the project data from the server project file overwrites the project data stored on the target server. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_revision.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing server project files to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to select which project <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="04c25ee8-212a-484d-90af-4b2f98c49629">revision or branch</ac:inline-comment-marker> should be <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f73966ba-649e-460b-8962-aa81f4aa7985">updated</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Skip</strong> - The same version of the project you're trying to import exists on the server. The existing project version on the server will be used in the project that requires it. No new data will be imported or updated.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Import</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />When the import is complete, the <strong>Notification Window</strong> opens saying that the projects were imported successfully.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003416 space=CM version=2 -->
## PAGE 00034: (2026x Refresh1) Importing the content of a used project into your project

- page_id: `304003416`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003416/2026x+Refresh1+Importing+the+content+of+a+used+project+into+your+project
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:23:17.174+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To import the content of a used project into your project, you must have the [**Administer Resources** permission](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/?contextKey=permissions&version=2026x Refresh1&variant=default).

If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.

Standard/system profiles, such as *UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip*, cannot be imported.

To import the contents of a used project into your project

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Locking model for edit' space=''] .
2. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. Select the used project and click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. Shortcut menuThis step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select **Import**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Import dialog, choose how to import the elements of the used project: with new IDs or with old.
5. If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click **Yes** and wait while the import completes. This may take some time. NoteThe imported project no longer appears in the **Project Usages** dialog.
6. Click OK .

The imported project also disappears from the package *Project Usages* and a new package named after the imported project appears under the root package in the Model Browser. If the imported project uses other projects, these projects will be directly used in your project.

After it is imported to the contents of your project, a used project will still exist in the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) repository.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a3d4aa3-1caa-4487-8226-47573b3c0e12"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Standard/system profiles, such as <em>UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip</em>, cannot be imported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To import the contents of a used project into your project</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong> Collaborate</strong> &gt; Project Usages &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the used project and click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac0e76a5-8ae3-4829-8bdb-a3a08b2944bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Shortcut menu</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select <strong>Import</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_usages_import.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>In the <strong>Import</strong> dialog, choose how to import the elements of the used project: with new IDs or with old.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click <strong>Yes</strong> and wait while the import completes. This may take some time.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17f6ab1e-9cfb-4f41-9fdf-79440af6a9a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The imported project no longer appears in the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The imported project also disappears from the package <em>Project Usages</em> and a new package named after the imported project appears under the root package in the Model Browser. If the imported project uses other projects, these projects will be directly used in your project.</p><p>After it is imported to the contents of your project, a used project will still exist in the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) repository.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003782 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00035: (2026x Refresh1) Importing the content of used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `304003782`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003782/2026x+Refresh1+Importing+the+content+of+used+projects+in+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:46.605+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > (2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages in 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.

Standard/system profiles, such as *UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip*, cannot be imported.

To import the contents of a used project into your project

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Locking models in 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''] .
2. In the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. In the **Project Usages** dialog, do one of the following:
  - Select a used project and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - Right-click a used project and select Import . [ATTACHMENT filename='importing_used_projects.png']
4. In the Import dialog, do one of the following:
  - Click the **Import with New IDs** button to create new IDs for the imported elements in the main project. This way, element IDs are not duplicated, ensuring the main project remains intact. However, if the elements of the used project are used in other projects, choosing **Import with****New IDs** may break those references. Importing elements with new IDs is highly recommended as it is the safest option regarding project consistency.
  - Click the **Import with Old IDs** button to import the elements with their existing IDs, which can be useful if the elements are used in multiple other projects. However, this may cause duplicate element IDs, resulting in the main project being corrupted. To proceed with the import using the old IDs, click **Yes**in the question****dialog, acknowledging that the procedure might corrupt the data of the main project.
5. If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click **Yes** and wait while the import completes. This may take some time.
6. Click OK to close the dialog .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">If you no longer need to keep a part of the model in the used project, you can import the content of that used project into your project. After the import, all the elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a3d4aa3-1caa-4487-8226-47573b3c0e12"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Standard/system profiles, such as <em>UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip</em>, cannot be imported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To import the contents of a used project into your project</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Locking models in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong> Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select a used project and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing the content of used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li>Right-click a used project and select <strong>Import</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_used_projects.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing the content of used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Import</strong> dialog, do one of the following: <ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import with New IDs</strong> button to create new IDs for the imported elements in the main project. This way, element IDs are not duplicated, ensuring the main project remains intact. However, if the elements of the used project are used in other projects, choosing <strong>Import with </strong><strong>New IDs</strong> may break those references.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f73d9e1f-6144-49a9-b69c-3c28a0861585"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Importing elements with new IDs is highly recommended as it is the safest option regarding project consistency.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import with Old IDs</strong> button to import the elements with their existing IDs, which can be useful if the elements are used in multiple other projects. However, this may cause duplicate element IDs, resulting in the main project being corrupted. To proceed with the import using the old IDs, click <strong>Yes </strong>in the question<strong> </strong>dialog, acknowledging that the procedure might corrupt the data of the main project.</p></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you receive a message asking to commit changes made in your project before the import, click <strong>Yes</strong> and wait while the import completes. This may take some time.</p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialog<strong>.</strong></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003801 space=CM version=2 -->
## PAGE 00036: (2026x Refresh1) Installing Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin

- page_id: `304003801`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003801/2026x+Refresh1+Installing+Cameo+Collaborator+Publisher+plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:23:19.329+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > (2026x Refresh1) Using Cameo Collaborator
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To publish Cameo Collaborator documents to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you must install the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin in your modeling tool.

#### WARNING: Compatibility

Compatibility

Make sure the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin version is compatible with the version of your modeling tool. See the [compatibility table](https://docs.nomagic.com/PC/?contextKey=product-compatibility&version=2022x-2024x%20Refresh3).

For information regarding installation, licensing, and system requirements, visit the[Installation, licensing, and system requirements](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=installation-licensing-and-system-requirements&version=2026x Refresh1)page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To publish Cameo Collaborator documents to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, you must install the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin in your modeling tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e30a40f-77ee-403b-876b-18c68c81d94f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin version is compatible with the version of your modeling tool. See the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/PC/?contextKey=product-compatibility&amp;version=2022x-2024x%20Refresh3">compatibility table</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>For information regarding installation, licensing, and system requirements, visit the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=installation-licensing-and-system-requirements&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Installation, licensing, and system requirements</a><span> page.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003394 space=CM version=2 -->
## PAGE 00037: (2026x Refresh1) Linking an embedded project with relevant server project

- page_id: `304003394`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003394/2026x+Refresh1+Linking+an+embedded+project+with+relevant+server+project
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-29T13:23:16.813+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Using Teamwork Cloud > (2026x Refresh1) Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To link an embedded project with relevant server project, you must have the [Administer Resources permission](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/?contextKey=permissions&version=2026x Refresh1&variant=default).

Linking replaces the privately embedded project (also referred to as the local project) with a relevant server project.

An embedded project appears in your project after you:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using server projects' space=''] in your project.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space=''] to Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) together with the main project.

The embedded project can be:

- Used (referenced) in the main project
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing the content of a used project into your project' space='']Importedinto the main project
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Stop using the project in your project' space='']

However, because it is not a server project, it has no versions and cannot be updated.

If a relevant project is stored on the server (it may be added to the server individually, separately from the project using it), you can easily link the embedded project with it.

To link an embedded project with a relevant server project

1. In the Model Browser, under Project Usages , right-click the embedded project.
2. Select Project Usages > Project Usage Options . The Project Usages dialog opens. The embedded project is already selected.
3. Click the Link with Server Project button (see the following figure). The Use Server Project wizard opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_usages_link_with_server_project.png']
4. The same steps of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using server projects' space=''] are outlined below.

The following figure highlights the differences between displaying an unlinked embedded project and one linked with the server project (converted to the used project).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Notation differences in Model Browser for embedded and used projects.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="660e2072-9c67-4bfd-a314-4d8ce037351b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To link an embedded project with relevant server project, you must have the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/?contextKey=permissions&amp;version=2026x Refresh1&amp;variant=default">Administer Resources permission</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Linking replaces the privately embedded project (also referred to as the local project) with a relevant server project.</p><p>An embedded project appears in your project after you:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using server projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Start using it]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in your project.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="usages"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Refuse to add a used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) together with the main project.</li></ul><p>The embedded project can be:</p><ul><li>Used (referenced) in the main project</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing the content of a used project into your project" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Imported </span>into the main project</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Stop using the project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Stopped being used]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>However, because it is not a server project, it has no versions and cannot be updated.</p><p>If a relevant project is stored on the server (it may be added to the server individually, separately from the project using it), you can easily link the embedded project with it.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>To link an embedded project with a relevant server project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, under <strong>Project Usages</strong>, right-click the embedded project.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt;<strong> Project Usage Options</strong>. The <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog opens. The embedded project is already selected.</li><li>Click the <strong>Link with Server Project</strong> button (see the following figure). The <strong>Use Server Project</strong> wizard opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_usages_link_with_server_project.png" /></ac:image></li><li>The same steps of<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using server projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ using a project in the server project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are outlined below.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>The following figure highlights the differences between displaying an unlinked embedded project and one linked with the server project (converted to the used project).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Differences in displaying embeded and used projects in the model" ac:alt="Differences in displaying embeded and used projects in the model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="representation_of_usages.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"> Notation differences in Model Browser for embedded and used projects.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304003857 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00038: (2026x Refresh1) Lock-Free Editing mode

- page_id: `304003857`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/304003857/2026x+Refresh1+Lock-Free+Editing+mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T11:11:52.358+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > (2026x Refresh1) Locking models in 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example #2

833100474

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example #2

833100476

833100473

**On this page**

**5**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example #2

833100475

The Lock-Free Editing mode, the default mode for the **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform, enables you to work with model elements without needing to lock them. Keep in mind that this is applicable to model elements only. Locks for diagrams, project options, and used projects will persist; you will still have to lock them before editing.

#### TIP: Recommendation

Recommendation

Assigning differentscopesof the model to various users is the recommended way to work in the Lock-Free Editing mode to prevent conflicts that may occur when users' changes interfere with each other.

Even though users usually work on different parts of the model, occasionally when they edit the same model elements conflicting changes may occur. In that case, you will be asked to review these conflicts in the**Conflicting Changes** panel and then take action, such as confirming the update result or discarding local changes.

#### TIP: Example #1

Example #1

*User 1 and User 2 are working on the same model iteration: [1] A.1. The model contains Class A.*

*User 1 deletes Class A from the model and commits the project to the server. Model iteration [1] A.2 is created.*

*User 2 renames Class A to Class B and commits/updates the project to the server. Conflicting changes occur.*

#### TIP: Example #2

Example #2

*User 1 and User 2 are working on the same model iteration: [1] A.1. The model contains Class A.*

*User 1 renames Class A to Class B and commits the project to the server. Model iteration [1] A.2 is created.*

*User 2 renames Class A to Class C and commits the project to the server. Conflicting changes occur.*

##### Conflicting Changes panel

To open the panel

1. In the main menu, click Window .
2. Select Conflicting Changes from the drop-down menu. The Conflicting Changes panel opens.
3. [ATTACHMENT filename='conflicting_changes_panel.png'] The Conflicting Changes panel.

Alternatively, you can open it by clicking the **Review Conflicts**button directly on the warning that appears when conflicting changes are detected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Conflicting Changes warning.

Please note that conflicting changes are checked upon a project update or a commit that requires an update.

##### Composition of the Conflicting Changes panel

The **Conflicting Changes** panel consists of three smaller panels that provide the following pieces of information to the user:

- Conflicting Local Changes : shows locally modified elements whose changes conflict with the changes already committed to the server.
- Upcoming Changes : shows the upcoming changes that conflict with the selected local change.
- Preview Update Result : shows the changes that will be applied after the project update.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Conflicting Changes panel informs users about the changes that conflict in the project.

##### Symbols used in the Conflicting Changes panel

| Icon | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Checks the presence of the conflicting changes in the model. |
|  | Selects the previous conflicting change on the list. |
|  | Selects the next conflicting change on the list. |
|  | Expands elements in the Conflicting Local Changes panel. |
|  | Collapses elements in the Conflicting Local changes panel. |
|  | Confirms the update result affecting the selected element. |
|  | Confirms all update results of the conflicting changes at once. |
|  | Filters confirmed/unconfirmed conflicting changes. |
|  | Shows that the conflicting change has been confirmed by the user. |

##### Resolving conflicting changes

**Confirming update results**

Once you have reviewed the conflicting changes, you can confirm the update result that will be applied after the project update. It should be emphasized that remote deletions always take precedence over local changes.

To confirm an update result

1. In the Conflicting Changes panel, do one of the following:
  - select the specific conflicting element and click [ATTACHMENT filename='confirm.png'] in the toolbar at the top.
  - click [ATTACHMENT filename='confirm_all_update_results.png'] in the toolbar to confirm all conflicting changes at once.

Right-click the conflicting element and select **Confirm Update Result**in the shortcut menu. The acknowledged change is marked with a green tick[IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Confirming the conflicting changes via the shortcut menu.

The following outlines another group of actions you can perform to handle detected conflicting changes:

- Select in Containment Tree : click to select the element in Containment tree.
- Quick Diff : select to inspect the conflicting changes in a compact form.
- Full Diff : select to inspect the conflicting changes in a detailed form.
- Compare Projects : select to compare the modified projects.
- Discard Changes : select to discard the local changes.

Please note that you cannot undo the discarded changes.

Depending on the action you have performed prior to the detection of conflicting changes in your model, you will see either the **Commit** or the **Update** button in the **Conflicting Changes** panel after confirming the conflicting changes in your project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Update or Commit button in the Conflicting Changes panel.

##### Disabling the Lock-Free Editing mode

To lock all lockable elements and work in a lock-required mode, disable lock-free editing.

To disable the Lock-Free Editing mode

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project .
2. In the panel on the left, select General > Collaboration .
3. Under the Locking options, disable Lock-Free Editing by setting the value to false , and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='disabling_lock_free_editing.png']
4. In the **Question** dialog asking whether you want to commit the changes to the server, select **Continue**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the Information dialog, click OK . The Lock-Free Editing mode is now disabled. [ATTACHMENT filename='information_panel_lock_required_mode.jpg']

If the Lock-Free Mode is disabled in the project, users who worked without locks will not be able to commit their changes to the server. We recommend making sure that all users working on the model do not have uncommitted changes before switching modes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
