<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=256442493 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00127: (2026x) Collaboration between disconnected teams

- page_id: `256442493`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/256442493/2026x+Collaboration+between+disconnected+teams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-15T09:44:32.564+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x) Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['disconnected-collaboration']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1177149143

1177149146

1177149144

Teamwork Cloud server allows for collaborating on projects, supporting multiple contractors who cannot work on the same Teamwork Cloud installation. There are two ways to collaborate on projects – using either the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using server project file format' space='']server project file format *.szip***or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using local project file format' space='']local project file format *.mdzip**.*

Read more about different ways to collaborate on projects in the following sections:

1177149142

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Saving a local copy of Teamwork Cloud project' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Branching projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca41bd61-e500-4e3f-b9ae-a24f0d7c5a53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1177149143</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e940427f-a8d9-4081-9e36-78aae150d698"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1177149146</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2941fd7a-1c8e-4e05-a3a2-55429caa4184"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1177149144</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Teamwork Cloud server allows for collaborating on projects, supporting multiple contractors who cannot work on the same Teamwork Cloud installation</span>. There are two ways to collaborate on projects – using either the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using server project file format" /><ac:link-body>server project file format <em>.szip</em></ac:link-body></ac:link><em> </em>or the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using local project file format" /><ac:link-body>local project file format <em>.mdzip</em></ac:link-body></ac:link><em>.</em></p><p>Read more about different ways to collaborate on projects in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="614d739d-186d-44cd-a020-9d85dfe18fa8" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="442bff62-59ea-48bb-b0a8-00397156cc11"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1177149142</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Saving a local copy of Teamwork Cloud project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Branching projects" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Model Merge" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=256442477 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00128: (2026x) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `256442477`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/256442477/2026x+Collaboration+powered+by+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-15T09:44:31.673+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

CATIA Magic and No Magic modeling tools support collaboration on UML/SysML v1/UAF models in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform (the feature also known as Power'By). You can use a modeling tool to create and store your projects in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform. Moreover, different team members can work on the same project simultaneously, commit new project iterations to the platform, and update the project when new iterations become available.

There are two ways you can start a collaboration session in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform:

- Open a modeling tool and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform .
- Use the Design with CATIA Magic app in the 3D EXPERIENCE platform to open a modeling tool installed on your computer.

600400

To learn how to collaborate on projects in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, see:

- If collaboration powered by the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform is enabled, you cannot work with Teamwork Cloud.
- Unsupported plugins:
  - Model Obfuscator.
  - Cameo Concept Modeller. Contact [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support' space='SUP'] for the workaround.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>CATIA Magic and No Magic modeling tools support collaboration on UML/SysML v1/UAF models in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(the feature also known as Power'By)</span>. You can use a modeling tool to create and store your projects in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform. Moreover, different team members can work on the same project simultaneously, commit new project iterations to the platform, and update the project when new iterations become available.</p><p>There are two ways you can start a collaboration session in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform:</p><ul><li>Open a modeling tool and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>log in to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Use the <a href="https://help.3ds.com/2023x/english/dsdoc/CATMagcUserMap/CATMagc-c-ov.htm?contextscope=cloud">Design with CATIA Magic app</a> in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform to open a modeling tool installed on your computer.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05e9fe92-b3a8-4879-a7b6-76cce3f26998"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="http://youtube.com/watch?v=52ANuF6gOH0" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br />To learn how to collaborate on projects in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, see:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4b769c12-280d-4052-b68e-48138a1c9b91" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29d89163-9955-4e7c-a561-e0ce0574f2b5"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If collaboration powered by the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform is enabled, you cannot work with Teamwork Cloud.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Unsupported plugins:</span><br /><ul><li>Model Obfuscator.</li><li>Cameo Concept Modeller. Contact <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SUP" ri:content-title="Support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Customer Support]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the workaround.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=256442660 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00129: (2026x) Committing changes to 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `256442660`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/256442660/2026x+Committing+changes+to+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-15T09:44:40.815+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x) Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After you finish editing a server project, you must commit the changes to the **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform so that other users working on the same project would be able to see them. When you commit project changes, new project data from your modeling tool is merged with the project stored on the**server**. As a result, a new project iterationis created.

To commit project changes to the **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Collaborate > Commit Changes to Server .
2. In the **Comment** box of the open dialog, optionally describe the model changes.
3. If you want to keep the locks on all diagrams, leave the option Keep locks selected.
4. If you want to view the local changes you are about to commit, click the**More**button. In the**Local Changes**tab, added elements are highlighted in green, deleted elements are highlighted in red, and modified or moved elements are highlighted in blue. [ATTACHMENT filename='commiting_project_to_server.png']
5. Click the Commit button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After you finish editing a server project, you must commit the changes to the <span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"><strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE </span>platform so that other users working on the same project <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="875c48d0-21f7-4e1d-b308-0a70c1a183d7">would be able to see them</ac:inline-comment-marker>. When you commit project changes, new project data from your modeling tool is merged with the project stored on the </span><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"><strong>server</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. As a result, a new project iteration </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">is created.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To commit project changes to the <span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"><strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE </span>platform</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Commit Changes to Server</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Comment</strong> box of the open dialog, optionally describe the model changes.</p></li><li>If you want to keep the locks on all diagrams, leave the option <strong>Keep locks </strong>selected.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>If you want to view the local changes you are about to commit, click the </span><strong>More</strong><span> button</span><span>. In the </span><strong>Local Changes</strong><span> tab, added elements are highlighted in green, deleted elements are highlighted in red, and modified or moved elements are highlighted in blue.</span><br /><br /></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="commiting_project_to_server.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Committing changes to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click the <strong>Commit</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=256442461 space=CM version=1 -->
## PAGE 00130: (2026x) Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `256442461`
- space_key: `CM`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/256442461/2026x+Committing+changes+to+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-15T09:44:31.325+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x) Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation > (2026x) Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

After you finish editing a model, you need to commit the changes to the server so that other team members can see these changes when editing other parts of the model. When you commit changes to the server, new data from your modeling tool is merged with the project on the server repository. As a result, a new version of that project is created. Other team members must [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Updating changes from Teamwork Cloud' space=''] from the server to see the committed changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The workflow of modifying and committing server projects.

To commit a project to Teamwork Cloud

1. In the main menu. select Collaborate > Commit Changes To Server (or press Ctrl+K).
2. If you want to describe the changes you made, enter a comment in the **Comment** box of the **Commit Changes to the Server**dialog. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] above the **Comment** box to choose a comment from the recently used comments list.
3. If you want to keep locks on model elements, select the **Keep locks** check box. Your choice will be remembered. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Optionally, click More in the lower left corner of the dialog and do one or both the following:
  - .
  - .
5. Click the Commit button.

While the project is being committed, you will see a dialog with a progress bar and your position in the commit queue as shown below.

When multiple projects are committed to the server simultaneously, all users are put in a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) line resulting in quicker and more predictable project commit execution. If putting project commits in a queue does not work for your organization, your system administrator can for a particular Teamwork Cloud server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The commit progress dialog with your position in the commit queue.

##### Creating version tags

You can create tags for a project version when you commit it to the server. Version tags provide valuable information when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reviewing historical versions of the project' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Branching projects' space='']. You can also use tags to trigger notifications when used project versions with specific tags are committed to the server. To learn more, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Changing used project versions' space=''].

To create a version tag and commit a project

1. In the main menu. select Collaborate > Commit Changes To Server (or press Ctrl+K).
2. In the lower left corner of the Commit Changes to the Server dialog, click More .
3. In the Version Tags tab, click the Add button.
4. In the open dialog, type the tag text and click OK . The tag is added to the Version Tags tab. You create several tags for one project version.Select the **Create major version** check box, to add the 'Major' tag to the project version. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_version_tags.png']
5. Click the Commit button.

##### Reviewing local changes before committing a project

You can review the local changes you have made to the project before committing it to the server.

To review local changes and commit a project

1. In the main menu. select Collaborate > Commit Changes To Server (or press Ctrl+K).
2. In the lower left corner of the Commit Changes to the Server dialog, click More .
3. Select the Local Changes tab and review local changes made to the project. Added elements are highlighted in green, deleted elements are highlighted in red, and modified or moved elements are highlighted in blue. [ATTACHMENT filename='reviewing_local_changes.png']
4. If you want to explore the changes of a certain element in more detail, select an element, click [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diff.png'] on the right side of the Local Changes tab, then select one of the following options:
  - Quick Diff - to quickly inspect the changes of element property values (such as documentation, type, or multiplicity).
  - Full Diff - to inspect the changes made to diagrams or element property values in more detail, as well as look at the properties of new relationships and directly related elements (such as new or modified attributes and operations).
  - Compare projects - to compare local project changes to the latest project version on the server. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing comparison results' space='MT'] .
5. Click the Commit button.

##### Disabling commit queues

If putting project commits in a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) line does not work for your organization, your system administrator candisable the commit queue mechanismfor a particular Teamwork Cloud server.

To disable commit queues

1. Open for editing the <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf file.
2. Add the following line to the file:
3. Restart the Teamwork Cloud server.
